title: Customer Service Representative
description:
Company: Green Can Cleaner Inc.

Location: Remote 

Schedule: Full-time, Monday through Friday, 9:00 AM to 6:00 PM Mountain Time (MT)

About Us

Green Can Cleaner is a Denver metro residential and commercial trash can cleaning service founded in 2018. We provide scheduled cleaning services that keep our customers' bins fresh, sanitary, and odor-free. With over 85,000 services completed and more than 1,500 five-star Google reviews, we've built a reputation for reliability and exceptional customer care. We're a stable, growing company looking for tea
mbers who share our commitment to quality service.

About the Role

We're looking for a Customer Service Representative to join our team as the frontline connection between our company and our customers. You'll handle everything from welcoming new customers to helping existing ones get the support they need. This is a year-round, full-time position with consistent hours and the opportunity to grow with us.

What You'll Do

Onboard new customers by setting up their accounts, answering questions about our services, and scheduling their cleanings. Follow up on unpaid invoices and assist customers in bringing their accounts current. Support the operations team by helping customers reschedule services when needed. Make warm outreach calls to customers who have used one-time services, inviting them to explore our recurring service options.

What We're Looking For

Fluent English skills, both written and verbal. You'll be communicating with U.S.-based customers daily. Comfortable with technology. We use Quo (formerly OpenPhone) for calls and texts, along with standard business software. Reliable high-speed internet connection and a quiet workspace for taking calls. Available to work U.S. Mountain Time hours: 9:00 AM to 6:00 PM, Monday through Friday. Warm, professional communication style with a problem-solving mindset.

Our Hiring Process

We take hiring seriously and want to make sure we're a good fit for each other. Our process includes written and verbal English assessment, skills testing relevant to the role, behavioral assessment, and technology and internet stability verification.

How to Apply

Submit a cover letter, resume, and a brief video introduction telling us why this role interests you and your favorite book you have read through this platform.

title: EA
description:
Job title: Executive Assistant — Inbox Management



Overview

Seeking a reliable, detail-oriented EA to manage my
 and communications remotely. Main focus: inbox triage, drafting replies, ensuring timely follow-ups, coordinating with staff, and maintaining inbox zero by archiving
s into client folders.



Key responsibilities



Monitor and triage Gmail/Outlook daily; flag urgent messages.

Draft, edit, and send professional responses using templates/guidance.

Archive
s into designated client folders and maintain inbox zero.

Communicate clearly with tea
mbers to action requests, relay messages, and coordinate tasks.

Label, organize, and apply filters; prioritize follow-ups.

Summarize long threads and note action items.

Handle basic calendar adjustments and scheduling as needed.

Maintain confidentiality and discretion.

Requirements



Strong written English and professional tone.

Experience managing professional inboxes or as a virtual EA.

Proficient with Gmail/Outlook, labels/folders, filters, templates, and Google/Outlook Calendar.

Able to communicate professionally with other staff and follow internal processes.

Reliable internet and agreed overlap hours for real-time replies.

Apply

Send to [your
]:



Short cover letter (200 words)

Resume/CV

One-sentence sample reply to: “Can we move our meeting from Tuesday 10 AM to Wednesday 3 PM?”

Notes

Paid trial (1–2 hours) may be required. Compensation and schedule negotiable.

title: SENIOR ACCOUNTANT - CPA REQUIRED
description:
Location: Philippines 

Reports to: Head of Accounting 

Employment Type: Full-Time Independent Contractor, Remote 



At Firm Five, we modernize bookkeeping and fractional controllership. We serve a client base of 

emerging brands with $2M–$50M annual revenue in Canada and the US, helping them not only 

maintain their financial reporting but also optimize and automate their financial processes. Our 

clients rely on us for all things accounting and administration (we don't do tax work), and we take 

pride in delivering clarity, efficiency, and financial insight. 



POSITION SUMMARY: 

We are looking for an experienced and strategic Senior Accountant that will handle the full 

accounting cycle for US and Canadian clients. Your primary responsibility is to complete all the 

accounting in QBO or Xero for clients in various industries on a monthly basis. 



COMPENSATION RANGE: 

PHP 90,000–110,000 per month, based on experience and skill level 

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES: 

-Chart of accounts setup 

-Bank reconciliations 

-Bank categorizations 

-Receipt management 

-Profit and loss, balance sheet, cash flow statement preparation 

-Accounting for sales tax (No actual tax filings) 

-Accrual accounting and balance sheet adjustments 

-Excellent client communication via slack and
 (in English) 

-Adjusting entries and COGS 

-Opening balances and catchup bookkeeping 



QUALIFICATIONS: 

-Bachelor's degree in Accounting 

-CPA designation in the Philippines 

-3-5 years experience managing full cycle accounting for a book of clients, ideally in an accounting or bookkeeping firm 

-E-Commerce or inventory experience preferred but not required 



CHARACTERISTICS TO SUCCEED: 

-Passionate about numbers, accounting, and client management 

-Excellent communication skills with a passion for simplifying financial data 

-Proven project management skills with an ability to withhold deadlines successfully 

-Dependable collaborator with a strong team-oriented approach 

-Thrives in a high-pressure environment while navigating many competing priorities 

-Reliable internet and work from home equipment (laptop, keyboard, mouse) 

-Backup power supply 



WHY YOU WILL LOVE WORKING WITH US: 

-Competitive Salary & Benefits 

-Early employee incentive plan linked to growth of the company 

-Fully Remote with Flexible Hours (9AM to 1PM EST + 4 flexi hours) 

-Paid Holidays & Vacation 

-Paid Sick/Personal Days 

-Growth Opportunities 

-Bi-Weekly pay direct to your bank account via Wise 

-No screen tracking tools 



OUR COMMITMENT TO EQUITY & INCLUSION: 

At Firm Five, we are committed to fostering a culture of equity, inclusion, and respect. We value 

diversity in all its forms and believe that every individual deserves to be treated with dignity and 

given equal opportunities to thrive. We strive to create an environment where all people feel 

welcome, supported, and empowered. We are dedicated to continuous learning, reflection, and 

improvement in our practices and policies.

title: Video Editor FULL-TIME | Founder Ads & Ecom UGC Ads
description:
About Us

Seek Jesus is a faith-driven apparel brand on a mission to build the largest community of Digital Disciples on the internet.

We design everyday essentials: hoodies, sweat sets, tees; built to be worn on repeat with Christ on display. Every piece we make carries a message. Every piece of content we publish carries that same weight.



We have 250K followers on Instagram and we relaunched three weeks ago under new ownership with one clear vision: become the most influential Christian apparel brand in the world. (While reaching 1,000,000 public testimonies and 10,000 evangelicals; making a full-time income talking about their faith).



Website: seekjesus.co



We are not a clothing company that happens to post on social media.

We are a movement that happens to sell clothes.

To get there, we are building a creator program of faith-aligned ambassadors posting daily content across TikTok and Instagram. The content engine behind that program needs to be world-class.



Who We Are Looking For

* Our top 1% applicant does not need to be told what good looks like.

* You have edited for creators before, real people on camera, not just branded studio content

* You communicate clearly in English, respond fast on Slack, hit deadlines without being chased, and treat feedback as information rather than criticism. You are available full-time during US Mountain hours and you show up to that commitment like a professional.

* You follow creators who are doing short-form at the highest level. You are always getting better.

You do not need to be a Christian to apply. But you need to respect what this brand stands for and bring your best to the content that carries it.



What You Will Edit

You will work directly with our Chief Creator editing three primary content types daily:

***Founder and talking head content, short and long-form videos featuring our founders and program leaders speaking directly to camera. Intentional pacing, B-roll layering, subtitle design, and cinematic treatment that makes a real person riveting to watch.

***UGC and creator content, TikTok affiliate-style videos from our ambassador creators. Fast cuts, trending audio, text overlays, hook-forward structure. Built to stop the scroll and drive to purchase.

***Cinematic brand and course content, longer evergreen videos for our creator program training and brand storytelling. Clean color grade, deliberate pacing, professional finish.



Portfolio Requirements

We want to see three things. Show us your actual best work, not your most recent.

***1. Founder Content, Required

A talking head or documentary-style edit featuring a personal brand figure or founder. Reference 
 (Robert Oliver) on TikTok and Instagram. We want to see intentional pacing, B-roll use, subtitle integration, and your ability to make a person on camera feel worth watching. Submit your single best example.

***2. UGC / TikTok Creator Style, Required

A short-form UGC or TikTok affiliate-style edit. Think TikTok Shop creator content, authentic, fast-paced, hook-forward, built to convert. This should feel native to the platform, not like a produced ad. Submit your single best example.

***3. Apparel Brand Ad, Bonus

A polished paid ad edit for a fashion or apparel brand. Cinematic color, clean transitions, lifestyle feel. This is not required but applicants who include a strong example here will be prioritized in our review.







Requirements

Tech Stack, Required

* Adobe Premiere Pro

* CapCut

* 

* Slack

* Google Meet

* Bonus - After Effects



How to Submit

: 

Subject line: Video Editor Application, [Your Name]



Include the following in your
:

* Link to your top 2 videos: one founder/talking head edit SIMILAR 
 and one UGC/TikTok style edit SIMILAR 
 only, no file attachments.

* Bonus: one apparel brand ad edit if you have it.

* A short paragraph (3–5 sentences) describing your editing process for a 60-second TikTok UGC video from raw footage to final delivery.



DO NOT send your entire portfolio with Google Drive. We will NOT review it. Only send 2-3 of your best work as we described (similar 
 
 not apply if you cannot show previous UGC or creator content editing experience. We will not respond to applications without portfolio links.



Availability

* Full-time, Monday through Friday

9am–5pm Mountain Standard Time (US hours, non-negotiable)

* Nearly fluent in English, written and spoken



Note on Our Hiring Process

* Review our website, instagram page and Liam Benson + Lena Oeun personal accounts. (You'll work closely with these two)

* We review all applications on a rolling basis. Shortlisted candidates will be invited to a 30-minute video call. The top 3 finalists will be given a paid test edit before any offer is made.

* We do not do unpaid trials. If you make our final three, we pay for your time.

* We are moving quickly. If this is you, apply now.

title: Leasing Specialist
description:
******TO BE CONSIDERED AS AN APPLICANT, PLEASE SUBMIT YOUR MOST UP-TO-DATE RESUME AND A VIDEO INTRODUCTION TAILORED TO THIS JOB POSTING!******



Leasing Specialist 



Job Type: Full-Time

Work Hours: Monday to Friday, 9:00 AM to 5:30 PM Central Time, with a 30-minute break

Salary: $1,000 per month



* Job Overview: 

We are seeking a proactive and detail-oriented Leasing Specialist to manage the full lead-to-lease process and support leasing operations. This role focuses on engaging prospective tenants, following up on leads, and guiding them through the leasing journey to conversion.

The ideal candidate is highly organized, responsive, and experienced in handling high volumes of inquiries across multiple channels. They should be comfortable working independently, maintaining accurate records, and ensuring a seamless experience from initial inquiry to signed lease.



* Key Responsibilities:

Manage and follow up on inbound leads from multiple platforms, including AppFolio, Rently, and Tenant Turner

Engage with prospective tenants via phone calls,
s, and text messages in a timely and professional manner

Track and document all interactions, updates, and progress within the CRM system

Nurture leads through the leasing funnel with the goal of converting prospects into residents

Coordinate with Property Managers to ensure smooth handoff for property showings

Prepare and send lease agreements to approved applicants

Maintain accurate and up-to-date records of all leasing activities



* Required Skills & Qualifications:

Proven experience in leasing, property management, or a similar customer-facing role

Background in residential property management (required)

Basic understanding of leasing processes and documentation (required)

Experience with AppFolio (required)

Familiarity with Rently and Tenant Turner (preferred)

Strong communication skills across phone,
, and SMS

Highly organized with strong attention to detail

Ability to manage multiple leads and follow-ups simultaneously

Self-starter with the ability to work independently in a remote environment



* Nice to Have: 

Experience in a lead-to-lease or leasing conversion-focused role





Note: An NBI or police clearance will be required upon acceptance of the job offer.

title: Graphic Production Assistant
description:
We are a Custom Sticker company. Customers upload their own images (logos, photos, artwork), and we turn those designs into vinyl stickers.



We are looking for a detail-oriented Production Artist to help convert customer uploads into clean, print-ready files.



This is not a creative design role. You will not be creating original designs. Instead, you will follow a clear, repeatable process to prepare files accurately and consistently.



What You’ll Do

- Clean up customer-uploaded images in Photoshop

- Remove backgrounds (including detailed edges like hair/fur)

- Improve image quality when needed (sharpening, contrast, etc.)

- Create cutlines in Illustrator (die cut, circle, square, etc.)

- Prepare print-ready files following our exact specifications

- Read and interpret customer instructions



Tools You’ll Use

- Adobe Photoshop

- Adobe Illustrator

title: Digital Filing Coordinator
description:
Caring employer seeking a reliable, long-term tea
mber.



This position provides critical support to our Finance Team through organized digital filing, document management, and administrative assistance. 



The role is heavy in Adobe use, PDF Editing and Merging, Sharepoint Folder Management, Teams Communication, digital organization,
 management, document processing, and data handling. You will manage and maintain digital filing systems, process documents, and support the team with various administrative tasks. 



We are looking for someone who is not afraid to ask questions, willing to take initiative, and comfortable wearing multiple hats. 



Our team is kind, patient, and very supportive. The environment is fast-paced and best suited for someone who can multi-task effectively.



Key Responsibilities:



Digital filing and organization of documents using Adobe Acrobat, SharePoint, and related tools

Managing and maintaining structured filing systems for contracts, invoices, pricing documents, and other critical records.





Required Skills:



Fluency in Adobe Acrobat (PDF creation, editing, combining, organizing)

Strong proficiency in SharePoint (document libraries, organization)

Advanced Outlook skills (inbox organization, rules, folders, professional
 communication)

Strong Excel skills (data entry, formulas, sorting, filtering, basic calculations)

Microsoft Teams proficiency



Ability to learn quickly and adapt to new processes



Industry: Industrial / Commodities / Recycling



Schedule:

5:30 AM – 1:30 PM PT (Pacific Time) – Monday to Friday (the hours can be adjusted after training period and based on business needs)



What We Offer:



Multiple performance evaluations and raises throughout the first year

Year-end bonus

Annual raises

Performance-based growth opportunities

Paid vacation time and PTO

Supportive, patient team environment

Salary negotiable based on experience



If you are organized, detail-oriented, tech-savvy with Microsoft and Adobe tools, and excited to grow with a dynamic team, we would love to hear from you!

title: Part-Time Bookkeeper / Tax Prep Support
description:
Position Type: Part-Time / As-Needed (Quarterly focus)

Rate: $4–$8/hour USD (based on experience)

Platform: QuickBooks Online

Location: Remote - Timezone flexible



About the Role

We're a small, fast-moving auto damage appraisal company based in Denver, Colorado (Elite Auto Estimates / Claim Factory) looking for a sharp, detail-oriented bookkeeper to support our business and owner's personal finances on an as-needed basis — primarily quarterly.

This is not a full-time role. We need someone who can jump in, knows exactly what they're doing, and doesn't need to be micromanaged. If you're the type who spots a missed deduction before we do, you're our person.



What You'll Be Doing



Reconciling accounts in QuickBooks Online (business + personal)

Categorizing transactions accurately

Preparing and organizing financial reports quarterly (P&L, balance sheet, cash flow)

Identifying and maximizing legitimate tax deductions for a small LLC

Organizing and maintaining clean financial records ahead of tax season

Coordinating with our US-based CPA as needed (documents, reports, summaries)

Flagging anything that looks off — we want proactive communication





What We're Looking For



Solid experience with QuickBooks Online (non-negotiable)

Familiar with US small business accounting and tax basics — deductions, LLC structure, Schedule C or S-Corp filing prep

Experience supporting a US-based client (not just Philippine accounting standards)

Strong attention to detail — mistakes here cost real money

Excellent written English communication

Self-starter who works independently without hand-holding

Responsive and reliable, even in a part-time capacity





Nice to Have



CPA or accounting degree

Experience with service-based businesses or contractors

Familiarity with separating business vs. personal expenses for a sole owner





How to Apply

To be considered, please send the following:



Your resume or a brief summary of your bookkeeping experience

A list of US-based clients you've supported (industry + scope, no names needed)

Your QuickBooks Online experience — how long, what you've done in it

Your available hours per week and time zone

Answer this question in 3–5 sentences: A small LLC owner uses one bank account for both business and personal expenses. How would you approach cleaning this up in QuickBooks Online?



Applications without #5 answered will not be reviewed. This is intentional — we want people who read carefully and can think through a real problem.



A Note on This Role

This starts small and as-needed. If you're reliable, communicative, and genuinely good at what you do, there's room for this to grow. We're building something long-term and want people who want to grow with us.

title: Google Ads Specialist — High-Ticket | US Digital Marketing Agency
description:

Google Ads Specialist — High-Ticket | US Digital Marketing Agency

Full-time

Remote

US hours

$1,000 – $1,800/mo

Immediate start

High-pressure, results-only role. Clients spend $5,000–$30,000/mo on ads. If you have never run successful campaigns for high-ticket products ($50,000+ deal sizes), do not apply. We verify every claim.

WHAT YOU'LL DO

—

title: Expert Long Form Video Editor, Motion Graphics, and Thumbnails
description:
Please look at Youtube: 
 before applying to make sure you can do the job!



Video Editor (Remote – Philippines)



Rate: $10/hour (USD)

Type: Part-time to full-time (Flexible Hours)

Company: Working with the creative teams behind 
 & 



About the Role



We’re seeking a highly skilled video editor to join our growing content and marketing team. You’ll be working across multiple online brands that create educational tutorials, customer testimonials, social media marketing videos, and long-form podcast content.



We’re looking for someone with the creative flair, speed, and professionalism of editors from an experienced agency

 — capable of turning raw footage into engaging, polished videos ready for YouTube, Instagram, TikTok, and other platforms.



Key Responsibilities



Edit high-quality tutorial and educational videos, including screen recordings, voiceovers, and motion graphics.



Produce customer testimonial videos that feel authentic and emotionally engaging (ideally identify short form clips from long form videos)



Create marketing and social content (ads, reels, promo clips, YouTube Shorts) optimized for performance across multiple platforms.



Edit and repurpose podcast episodes into full-length videos, highlight clips, and thumbnails for various social platforms.



Add captions, sound design, B-roll, and transitions to enhance storytelling.



Collaborate with our creative and marketing teams to maintain consistent branding and style.



Manage multiple projects with fast turnaround times while maintaining exceptional quality.



Requirements



You'll be working with files in 4K resolution & we expect 4K output

title: Executive Assistant

About the Role

What You Will Be Doing

Who We Are Looking For

Non-Negotiable Requirements

Graduate of a top Philippine university: University of the Philippines (UP), Ateneo de Manila University, De La Salle University, or University of Santo Tomas

Strong academic performance — preferably with Latin Honors or equivalent distinction

Proven experience supporting C-level executives directly

Why This Role

You will work closely with leadership, gain exposure to executive decision-making, and play a critical role in ensuring operational excellence across the business.

title: Full-Cycle Video Producer (Canva/CapCut/Premiere) – HVAC/Roofing FULL-TIME 9-5 EST
description:
We are seeking a highly detail-oriented Video Producer to manage end-to-end short-form video creation for HVAC clients.

This role goes beyond basic editing — we need someone who can research, script, source footage, and produce polished final videos with AI voiceovers and dynamic captions.



YOUR RESPONSIBILITIES:

-Creative Scripting: Draft engaging scripts based on a provided hook and call-to-action (CTA). Structure videos for high engagement and lead generation.

-Advanced Sourcing: Research client websites to find and scrape footage from their
, Instagram, TikTok, and YouTube.

-Asset Collection: Source HVAC equipment photos from our brand folders (Canva or Google Drive) or manufacturer websites.

-Local B-Roll Research: Find relevant city and service-area footage from Google or YouTube.

-Voiceover & Audio Production: Use ElevenLabs for all voiceovers. You must be experienced in voice grooming (phonetic spelling, aliases, or dashes) to ensure zero mispronunciations.

-Technical Accuracy: Ensure correct pronunciation of HVAC terms such as SEER, AFUE, BTUs and specific city or service area names.

-Dynamic Captions: Create word-for-word captions highlighted in Yellow and positioned in safe zones (mid-screen) so they are not covered by social media interface elements.



QUALITY & PAYMENT POLICY:

-Accuracy Standard: You are expected to deliver a high-quality first draft. Revisions required due to failure to follow instructions (incorrect phone number, wrong caption color, mispronounced terms, missing assets, etc.) must be corrected promptly.

-Billable Work: Additional payment will only be provided if the client changes the original scope or script after the first approved version.



REQUIREMENTS:

-Expert-level CapCut or Premiere Pro skills.

-Strong Canva experience.

-Pro-level familiarity with ElevenLabs including aliases, phonetic spelling, and pronunciation control.

-Strong English writing skills for technical HVAC scripting.

-Ability to deliver final video assets in three formats:

9:16 (Vertical)

1:1 (Square)

16:9 (Landscape)



HOW TO APPLY:

-Please start your proposal with "HVAC YELLOW" to verify you have read the full brief. 

-Provide 2–3 samples of your best short-form video work featuring dynamic captions and AI voiceovers.

-IF do not have portfolio showcasing work in our niche or prior experience creating HVAC/Plumbing or Roofing video ads, we’d like you to complete a short test project so we can properly evaluate your design skills, creativity, ability to follow directions, and understanding of ad structure.

-ASK: SEND ME TEST

- DO NOT submit your resume or any Figma or websites files as these are not considered as proof of your qualifications. SEND ONLY google drive or canva links to your previous work.

title: Lead UI/UX Designer – EduTrack
description:
About EduTrack

EduTrack is a growing EdTech platform focused on building smart, intuitive systems for schools and therapy-based organizations. Our mission is to create powerful tools that actually make life easier for administrators, educators, and clinicians.

We are looking for a Lead UI/UX Designer to take full ownership of the user experience and interface design across the platform.



Role Overview

As the Lead UI/UX Designer, you will own the entire UI/UX function of EduTrack. You will be responsible for translating real user needs into intuitive, clean, and scalable product designs. This is a key leadership role with real influence on product direction.

You will work closely with product, development, and leadership to shape how EduTrack looks, feels, and works.



Key Responsibilities

* Own and lead all UI/UX design for the EduTrack platform

* Deeply understand and analyze end-user needs (administrators, educators, therapists, parents, etc.)

* Translate complex workflows into simple, intuitive user experiences

* Design wireframes, user flows, prototypes, and high-fidelity UI designs

* Maintain and evolve the design system and product consistency

* Collaborate with developers to ensure accurate implementation

* Continuously improve UX based on feedback and real usage



Requirements

* Minimum 2+ years experience as a Lead / Senior UI/UX Designer

* Proven experience owning UI/UX projects end-to-end

* Very strong proficiency in Figma (must be expert level)

* Strong understanding of UX research, user journeys, and usability principles

* Experience working on complex systems (SaaS / dashboards / admin systems is a big plus)

* Ability to think both strategically and practically

* Strong communication and leadership skills



Nice to Have (Bonus)

* Experience in EdTech, HealthTech, or workflow-heavy systems

* Experience building design systems from scratch

* Understanding of accessibility and usability standards



What Makes This Role Special

* You are not “just a designer” — you own the product experience

* Real influence on a growing platform used by real organizations

* Opportunity to build something from the ground up

* Work directly with leadership and product vision



How to Apply

* Subject Line: Application for UI/UX Designer

* Attach your resume and links to project examples (must include SaaS and/or admin UI examples)

* Include your
 and onlinejobs.ph profile

* Mention your desired hourly salary range

description:

CRO Shopify Web Developer – Job Overview

I am looking for an experienced shopify web developer to help me upgrade client’s checkout processes and execute CRO tests for various ecommerce brands.

In this role you will update Shopify theme templates, develop landing pages using various page builders and html/CSS frameworks, research/install various checkout/bundle applications, and implement A/B tests using testing tools like intelligems.

You will also report on test performance, and provide suggestions for next steps.

This is a full-time opportunity. Training will be provided and you will work directly with me.



About Zack Miller

My name is Zack Miller, and I’ve been a DTC Ecommerce Growth Marketer since 2019. I’ve done everything fro
dia buying and client strategy, to agency internal operations.

I’ve contributed to the growth of over 200 ecommerce brands in my agency experience. Now, as an independent growth marketing partner, I work closely with a small number of brands on their new customer acquisition strategy.



Note: All Remote Employees Must Be Available To Work During The Following Business Hours:

Monday: 9am – 5:30pm EST

Tuesday: 9am – 5:30pm EST

Wednesday: 9am – 5:30pm EST

Thursday: 9am – 5:30pm EST

Friday: 9am – 5:30pm EST



Roles & Responsibilities

- Develop landing pages using Figma and various WYSYWIG editors including but not limited to Replo and Pagefly.

- Edit product, collection and other page templates using Shopify Liquid template engine, and various HTML/CSS frameworks.

- Install various checkout/tracking applications (aftersell, Zipify OCU, Upstack, etc)

- Install various applications for product bundling when needed, and create product bundles. 

- Install tracking pixels on client’s websites

- Implement A/B tests in intelligems for product price tests, shipping price tests, theme tests, landing page tests, and more. 

- Research new ideas for direct response content marketing strategies.

- Work in my project management system, ClickUp.

- Apply a process-first mentality and provide feedback to help further develop our operating systems.



Required Experience & Education

- Strong verbal and written English language skills (American Dialect)

- 1+ years of web development (Shopify) experience required

title: Video editor for a dtc ecom brand (meta ads)
description:
Video Editor – DTC Golf Training Brand (Paid Social Ads)

We're a growing direct-to-consumer golf training brand running paid social on Meta. We're scaling our ad creative and need a video editor who can turn raw footage into scroll-stopping ad content that actually converts.

What you'll be doing:



Editing short-form video ads for
 and Instagram (Reels, feed, Stories)

Cutting raw UGC and POV-style footage into multiple ad variations — hook swaps, length tests, CTA variations

Adding text overlays, captions, and simple motion graphics where needed

Working from creative briefs and iterating quickly based on performance feedback

Maintaining a fast turnaround — we test a lot of creative and move quickly

Organizing and managing raw footage and project files



You're a good fit if you:



Have experience editing direct response or performance ad creative (not just brand/lifestyle content)

Understand the native, organic look that works on Meta — you know how to keep ads from looking like ads

Can work in Premiere Pro, DaVinci Resolve, CapCut, or similar

Have a feel for pacing, hooks, and what makes someone stop scrolling

Can take a single piece of raw footage and cut multiple variations without being told exactly how

Are comfortable with high volume and quick iterations — this isn't one polished video a month



Nice to have:



Experience in ecommerce or DTC brands specifically

Familiarity with golf, sports, or fitness content

Basic motion graphics or After Effects skills

Understanding of split testing and why we create multiple versions of the same ad



Details:



Ongoing work, starting project-based with potential to move to a retainer

$7USD per video

Please link to 2–3 examples of short-form ad creative you've edited — UGC-style or direct response preferred over cinematic or corporate work

title: Technical Project Manager (Remote / Work From Home)
description:
PLEASE NOTE: WE ARE MOVING QUICKLY AND AIM TO HIRE IN THE NEXT TWO WEEKS.



About the Role



We are seeking a Technical Project Manager with strong experience leading software and cloud-based projects. You will help manage existing AI, web, and AWS/serverless initiatives while coordinating with internal teams, developers, and external stakeholders to keep projects moving on time and with clear accountability.



You should be comfortable translating business needs into concrete plans, organizing delivery across multiple workstreams, and identifying risks before they become real problems. This role is ideal for someone who is organized, proactive, technically curious, and strong in client communication.



Key Responsibilities



- Own day-to-day project delivery across software, AI, and cloud-based initiatives.

- Build and maintain project plans, timelines, priorities, and status updates.

- Run standups, planning sessions, retrospectives, and stakeholder check-ins.

- Coordinate internal developers, designers, QA, and outside vendors.

- Track risks, blockers, dependencies, and scope changes, and escalate issues early.

- Turn business discussions into actionable tickets, requirements, and next steps.

- Ensure work is delivered on time, within scope, and aligned with business priorities.

- Support release planning, UAT coordination, and production rollout readiness.

- Maintain strong project documentation across tools such as Jira, ClickUp, Notion, or similar systems.



Core Requirements



- 3+ years of experience managing software, technical, or digital projects.

- Strong written and verbal English communication skills.

- Experience working with cross-functional teams in a remote environment.

- Ability to manage multiple moving parts without losing control of details.

- Strong understanding of Agile delivery, sprint planning, and project tracking.

- Comfort working with engineers and discussing technical issues at a practical level.



Preferred Skills



- Experience managing web application or SaaS projects.

- Exposure to AWS, APIs, serverless systems, or AI-related projects.

- Experience with Jira, ClickUp, Asana, Trello, or similar PM tools.

- Ability to write clear requirements, meeting notes, and follow-up summaries.

- Experience working directly with US-based clients or stakeholders.



Compensation & Perks



- Monthly Rate: $1,400 - $1,900 USD (paid via Wise).

- Performance Bonus: Discretionary year-end performance bonus based on results and delivery.

- Direct US Client: No agency middleman taking a large cut of your fees.

- Mentorship: Work directly with senior technical leadership and gain real exposure to software, AI, and cloud delivery.

- Engagement Type: Independent contractor engagement. You are responsible for your own tax filings and social contributions.



How to Apply (Read Carefully)



To ensure your application is processed, do not apply through this site.
 us directly:



careers [at] diverseprogrammers [dot] com



Your
 must follow this exact format:



Subject Line:

- Result of "8 + 7" followed by your Name

- Example: 15 - Juan Dela Cruz



Body:

- The "PM" Question: In 2-3 sentences, what is the most common reason software projects fall behind, and how do you prevent it?

- Experience Summary: Briefly describe the most complex project you managed, including team size and your role.

- Links: LinkedIn profile and any portfolio, project summaries, or case studies.

- Resume: Attach your resume.

- Speed Test: Include a screenshot of your internet speed test.

title: Senior US Bookkeeper & Agency Partner (Fixed Salary + 50/50 Split)
description:
THE OPPORTUNITY: Own Your Own Portfolio

We are not looking for a traditional "data entry" employee. We are building a high-level US Bookkeeping Agency focused on the home service industry (Chimney Sweeps, HVAC, Window Cleaning, etc.), and we are looking for an Agency Partner to manage and grow their own client portfolio.



COMPENSATION STRUCTURE:

To provide stability while rewarding high performance:



There will be a fixed salary plus a 50/50 split after business expenses.



The fixed salary ensures you are compensated for your administrative and marketing efforts.



The 50/50 split (calculated after a 20% company operating fee) ensures you build long-term wealth as you grow your portfolio.



Income Potential: An organized partner managing a full portfolio of 50 clients can realistically earn $2,500 - $4,000+ USD per month.



YOUR ROLE (The Partner):

Portfolio Management: Perform full-cycle bookkeeping for a dedicated territory of US-based clients.



Accuracy & Deadlines: Ensure all books are audit-ready and 100% completed by the 10th day of the following month.



Growth & Marketing: Utilize our Marketing Vault 
, LinkedIn, and
 strategies) to acquire and manage clients in your assigned territory.



Wholesale Support: Act as the primary point of contact for US-based Accounting Firms (Wholesale Partners) that outsource their back-office work to us.



OUR ROLE (The Agency):

Infrastructure: We provide the GoHighLevel CRM, QuickBooks subscriptions, and all necessary software tools.



Protection: We provide professional liability and cyber insurance for the agency.



Management: Our management team handles technical QA (Quality Assurance) and higher-level administrative support.



Operations: We handle all U.S. legalities, corporate taxes, and ensure your payouts are made promptly via Wise or PayPal.



REQUIREMENTS:

US Expertise: Minimum 3+ years of experience in US-based bookkeeping, including deep knowledge of 1099s, Sales Tax, and the US Tax Calendar.



Volume Experience: Proven ability to manage a high volume of clients (20+) simultaneously without a drop in quality.



Tech Mastery: Expert level in QuickBooks Online (QBO Certified ProAdvisor preferred) and familiarity with GHL or similar CRMs.



Entrepreneurial Mindset: You must want to be a partner who is rewarded for the results you produce rather than just the hours you work.



HOW TO APPLY:

Provide a summary of your experience managing US-based bookkeeping clients.



What is the maximum number of clients you have managed at one time?



Confirm you understand the compensation model: Fixed salary + 50/50 split after business expenses.



Why do you prefer a profit-sharing partnership over a standard hourly wage?

title: Construction Purchasing Agent / Specialist-WFH
description:
Job Summary

The Purchasing Agent / Specialist is responsible for procuring goods and

services—ranging from raw materials and equipment to office supplies—at the most

competitive prices without sacrificing quality. This role involves managing vendor

relationships, negotiating contracts, and analyzing market trends to maintain a lean and

efficient supply chain.

Key Responsibilities

? Sourcing & Procurement: Identify and evaluate potential suppliers based on

price, quality, service, and reliability.

? Negotiation: Draft and negotiate contracts, including pricing, delivery schedules,

and payment terms to maximize value.

? Purchase Order Management: Prepare, process, and track purchase orders

(POs) through the entire lifecycle to ensure timely delivery.

? Vendor Relations: Maintain strong relationships with existing vendors and

conduct regular performance reviews to ensure compliance with SLAs.

? Market Analysis: Monitor industry trends and price fluctuations to anticipate

supply shifts or cost-saving opportunities.

? Inventory Coordination: Work closely with the warehouse and production

teams to ensure stock levels are optimized and avoid shortages.

? Compliance & Records: Maintain accurate records of purchases, pricing, and

inventory while ensuring all activities meet company and legal standards.

Required Skills & Qualifications

Feature Requirement

Education Bachelor’s degree in Business, Supply Chain Management, or a



related field.



Experience 2–5 years of experience in procurement, purchasing, or supply chain.

Technical

Skills



Proficiency in ERP software (SAP, Oracle, NetSuite) and Microsoft

Excel.



Soft Skills Strong negotiation, analytical thinking, and time-management skills.

Certifications CPSM (Certified Professional in Supply Management) or CPP



(Certified Purchasing Professional) is a plus.

title: Front End Full Stack Developer
description:
We are looking for a highly detail-oriented Shopify Full Stack Developer to join our growing front end team.



This role is ideal for someone who has strong hands-on experience working inside Shopify backend operations and enjoys testing websites, catching issues, and making sure pages are flawless before launch.



Ideal candidates can work PST hours, starting between 9am-10am PST and ending between 5pm and 6pm PST.



You will be a key part of our website and funnel launch process, helping ensure every landing page, and checkout flow works perfectly before going live.



This is a long-term remote contractor opportunity with a fast-paced beauty and ecommerce brand.



What You’ll Be Responsible For

-Build and maintain high-performance pages. You will be responsible for designing and deploying 2–3 pages per week using Liquid.

-Help implement split tests using Intelligems or similar A/B testing frameworks.

-Design, develop, and maintain robust and scalable software applications using modern programming languages and frameworks.

-Architect and implement efficient, secure, and high-performance solutions that align with business needs.

-Troubleshoot and resolve complex technical issues across the software stack.

-Optimize applications for performance, scalability, and security.

-Ensure adherence to best practices, including code quality, accessibility, and compliance with security standards.

-Collaborate with cross-functional teams, including media buyers, creative strategists and copywriters.

-Stay updated with emerging technologies and industry trends, incorporating them into development strategies when relevant.

-Contribute to DevOps practices, including CI/CD pipelines, cloud deployments, and infrastructure as code.



You will own the final quality check before pages go live.

This includes testing:

-landing pages

-product pages

-offer pages

-funnel pages

-cart pages

-checkout pages



You will verify:

-copy is correct

-images and product carousels display properly

-buttons and links work

-products add to cart correctly

-discounts apply correctly

-mobile and desktop functionality

-checkout flow works smoothly

-upsells and post-purchase offers work as intended



Experience with any of the following is highly preferred:

Loop subscriptions

Abra

Rocked

pre-purchase and post-purchase upsells

auto add-to-cart promotions

Shopify apps and integrations

ecommerce funnel QA



Important Responsibilities:

Run full QC checks before launch

Make test purchases and validate order flow

Flag bugs and critical issues

Communicate issues clearly to the team

Help ensure a smooth customer experience

Support fast-moving launches and promotions



What We’re Looking For:

Strong Shopify backend experience

Ecommerce website QA / testing experience

Extremely detail-oriented

Able to catch small issues before they become customer-facing

Strong written English

Reliable and responsive

Comfortable working independently

Experience supporting US-based ecommerce brands is a plus



Why This Role Matters:

This role is critical to helping us launch pages, and making split tests on liquid pages without errors. We’re looking for someone who takes pride in finding issues before our customers do.

title: Bookkeeper - QuickBooks & Stripe
description:
Hello! We are looking for a reliable Bookkeeper to join our team. 



CORE REQUIREMENTS:



1. Experience & Technical Skills

- 2+ years of bookkeeping or accounting experience

- Proven hands-on experience with QuickBooks (Online preferred)

- Experience working with Stripe or similar payment processors (Square, PayPal, etc.)



Strong understanding of:

- Bank and credit card reconciliations

- Chart of accounts setup and maintenance

- Journal entries and adjustments

- Ability to sync and troubleshoot integrations between Stripe and QuickBooks





2. Stripe-Specific Knowledge (This is key):

- Understand how Stripe payouts work (gross vs net deposits)



- Ability to properly account for:

Processing fees

Refunds and disputes

Transfers and payout timing



- Experience reconciling:

Stripe reports ? bank deposits ? QuickBooks entries





3. QuickBooks Responsibilities:

- Maintain accurate and up-to-date books

- Categorize transactions correctly

- Perform monthly reconciliations



Generate financial reports:

- Profit & Loss

- Balance Sheet

- Cash Flow (if needed)

- Clean up messy or miscategorized books 





4. Attention to Detail:

- High accuracy in data entry and reconciliation

- Ability to catch discrepancies (missing deposits, duplicate entries, etc.)

- Consistent documentation of processes





5. Organization & Communication:

- Meets deadlines (monthly close, reporting dates)

- Communicates clearly about issues or inconsistencies

- Can explain financials in simple terms (huge plus)





6. Bonus Qualifications (Nice to Have):

- Certified QuickBooks ProAdvisor

- Experience with e-commerce or high transaction volume businesses



- Familiarity with:

Sales tax tracking

Inventory 



- Experience cleaning up books for audits or tax prep and also...Wave Experience (or Ability to Learn Quickly)

Experience with Wave Accounting (invoicing, payments, basic reporting)





7. Ability to:

- Categorize transactions and reconcile accounts

- Manage invoices and customer payments

- Generate basic financial reports

- Understand differences between Wave and QuickBooks (since Wave is more limited)



HOW TO APPLY: 

When submitting application here, put "PINEAPPLE" word on the subject. 



Thank you!

 the above two items to

Company: Achieve Integrative Health, No. 1 Acupuncture Clinic in Cedar Park and Austin surrounding areas

Website: 
/



About the Company:

Join Achieve Integrative Health, Austin, TX's premier acupuncture clinic, and be part of a transformative healthcare journey. For over 18 years, we've championed health restoration, empowering thousands to reclaim their energy, vitality, and joy. Our success stems from a unique blend of Traditional Chinese Medicine and advanced technologies.



At Achieve Integrative Health, we're not just about healthcare; we're about life-changing care. Join us to deliver unparalleled service and results, reshaping healthcare one patient at a time. Let's make lives great again, together!



Let's build the future together! Your skills, combined with our vision and aligned with our values, will pave the path for a brighter tomorrow.



Why Join Us?

• Transform Lives: We're not just another company. At Achieve Integrative Health, WE MAKE a real difference by bringing joy and health back into lives with every interaction.

• Unbounded Growth: There is no limit to your growth with our company. We're on a mission to grow, with plans to expand our healing touch in Austin, Texas and its surrounding areas.

• Empowerment Team Culture: We are known for our team culture because we make it our mission to help our tea
mbers achieve their professional, financial, and personal goals. 



We're Seeking Individuals Who Are Aligned with Our Core Values...

• Service Above Self: You have a CARING heart and a HUMBLE attitude to put patients’ needs before yours.

• Commitment: You are persistent and disciplined in reaching your OWN goals that are demonstrated by your consistent performance and growth.

• Accountable: No excuses allowed. You are responsible for everything that happens to you and everything that doesn’t happen to you. You are responsible for everything that you have and everything that you don’t have.

• Results Oriented: While we acknowledge your effort, you are only rewarded when producing results that are measured via the statistics for your positions and for the company.

• Transparency: You maintain and practice clear, honest, and direct communication about your thoughts and intentions with every tea
mber and every patient.

• Inspirational: You lead by example to inspire our patients to live a healthy lifestyle by living a healthy lifestyle yourself and you lead by example to inspire each tea
mber by constantly striving to reach your own potential.



WHAT WE OFFER:

• Competitive total Compensation: Base salary + performance bonuses in the acupuncture industry that rewards you based on the RESULTS you produce. If you are looking for a guaranteed salary w/o being accountable to meeting KPIs, this is not the team for you.

* A team and environment that is collaborative, accountable, caring, supportive, honest, transparent, as we remove all employees who talk behind peoples back and lack integrity

• Learning & Growth Opportunities: Access a wealth of resources to keep sharpening your mindset, communication, and customer service skills.

• Wellness Initiatives: Benefit from complimentary virtual health services, ensuring your well-being is always a priority.

• Work from Home: Be able to build a career working from home, no more 3-4 hour commutes to work.

• Other Benefits: Paid-Time off and medical insurance for qualified tea
mbers after successfully completing the 90-day training period.



KEY RESPONSIBILITIES:

* assist the Chief Marketing Officer in reviewing
s, marketing material, uploading videos onto social media

* use Editing Apps that integrate AI (ie. CapCut, Canva, ChatGPT) to generate thumbnails, images, reels

* organize all of our marketing tasks and make sure all tasks are executed in a timely manner w/ excellence

* respond to social media comments

* create newsletters

* track All marketing stats

* Transcribe videos into
s and blogs

* vitally important = be able to follow the CMO's instructions

* and additional admin tasks



CHARACTER REQUIREMENTS & QUALIFICATIONS

• You must truly have a caring heart and truly want to help people improve their health, if you are mainly focused on how much money you can make, we are not the team for you

• You must be organized and be able to handle multiple tasks and responsibilities everyday. If you’re looking to only do one or two tasks per day, we are the wrong team for you.

• You must be able to follow instructions exactly as written, if you don’t like reading or following instructions, we are not the right team for you

• You must value Results over effort. If you like to be paid based on how many tasks you completed or how much responsibilities you have, we are not the team for you. We pay ONLY based on you meeting your KPIs, metrics, quotas.

• You must sleep the same schedule 7 days a week. Your Health is our priority, and if you don’t value your own health we don’t want you on our team.

• You must be honest and have integrity. If you are caught lying, you will be immediately terminated.

• You must want to EARN your pay (ie. base salary + bonuses) instead of being given a pay (ie. focused only on base salary) that you didn’t earn.

COMPENSATION AND BENEFITS:

mbers

title: Cold Caller — US Market | High Volume | Real Sales Only
description:
This is not a customer support job. This is not a virtual assistant job. This is pure outbound cold calling to business owners in the United States.

If you have done real cold calling, smashed daily dial targets, and thrive on rejection — read on. If you are looking for an easy work from home job, stop here.



What you will be doing:

Calling business owners in Florida — plumbers, HVAC companies, and garage door operators — and getting them on a demo call with our founder. That is your one job. Dial, pitch, book. Every single day.

We use a power dialer. You will hit 200 plus dials per day. The script is tight, the leads are pre-qualified, everything is set up. You bring the confidence and the hunger.



You are the right person if:



You have at least 1 year of outbound cold calling to US businesses

You have consistently hit 150 plus dials per day in a previous role

You are comfortable getting hung up on 50 times and picking the phone straight back up

You do not need to be managed to perform

You treat objections as opportunities not reasons to give up

You send a message every morning saying ready and every evening saying done





You are the wrong person if:



You have only done inbound or customer support

You need a script in front of you to think

You go quiet mid shift without warning

You have never been measured on dials or bookings per day

You need motivation to pick up the phone





Shift hours:

You will be working Florida business hours. Exact shift details shared upon hiring. You confirm ready every morning before your first dial and done every evening after your last one. No exceptions.



What we offer:



Competitive hourly rate based on experience

Power dialer fully set up — 200 plus dials per day, no manual dialing

Pre-qualified lead lists loaded and ready

Script, objection handlers, and training videos provided before day one

Performance bonuses for top bookers

Direct line to the founder — no layers of management

Fast growing company with real room to grow





To apply you must do three things:

1. Answer these two questions in 3 sentences max each:



What is your highest dial day ever and what was the result?

Give me your exact word for word opener on a cold call to a business owner you have never spoken to before.



2. Record a 90 second cold call sample. You are calling a plumber in Florida named Mike. He owns a 3 truck operation and you need to get him on a 10 minute call with the founder of a SaaS company. Mike is skeptical, busy, and has heard pitches before. Do not ask for permission to speak. Do not introduce yourself before asking a question. Do not mention price. Just get him to say yes to a meeting.

3. Send both with your application.

No voice sample, no response. No answers to both questions, no response. Generic applications are deleted without reading.

We are moving fast. The right person starts this week.

title: ADMINISTRATIVE ASSISTANT & BOOKKEEPER
description:
We are a fast-growing construction and real estate property management company seeking a highly organized, detail-obsessed Virtual Administrative Assistant & Bookkeeper. This is not a passive support role — you are the operational backbone of both companies.



You will own the books, manage communications, support our project managers, coordinate tenant and guest communications, and ensure nothing falls through the cracks. If you thrive in a fast-paced environment, take ownership without being asked, and are passionate about accuracy and systems, this role is built for you.



KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

•	Phones & Communications: Answer, screen, and route all incoming calls and communications to the appropriate tea
mber or department with professionalism and urgency.

•	Bookkeeping: Manage and maintain the books for both entities in QuickBooks Online — journal entries, reconciliations, P&L reporting, and chart of accounts hygiene.

•	Payroll: Process bi-weekly payroll for all employees; coordinate with ownership on adjustments, bonuses, commissions, or special pay items.

•	Accounts Payable: Track all vendor invoices and subcontractor payments; ensure bills are coded correctly, approved on time, and paid by due date.

•	Project Support: Assist project managers with scheduling, job file documentation, client communication, subcontractor coordination, and milestone tracking.

•	Property Management: Manage tenant communications, lease tracking, maintenance request coordination, and rental reporting across our property portfolio.

•	Inventory Management: Track materials, supplies, and equipment across job sites and the office; flag low-stock items and coordinate reorders.

•	Accounts Receivable: Process customer payments through Jobtread, reconcile with QuickBooks, and follow up on outstanding balances.

•	Project & Sales Support: Support the sales team and project team on as needed help or assignments.



QUALIFICATIONS & REQUIREMENTS

•	Attention to Detail: Non-negotiable. You catch errors before they become problems. Deadlines, decimal points, and
 response times are things you take personally.

•	Ownership Mindset: You run the office. You don't wait to be told — you identify gaps, fix them, and report back. This role demands initiative, not task-completion.

•	Experience: Minimum 3 years of bookkeeping or administrative experience. Prior experience supporting a construction or property management business is a strong plus.

•	English Proficiency: Excellent written and spoken English required. You will represent the company on calls and in client-facing communications.

•	Home Office: Reliable internet, professional workspace, and backup power preferred. Must be fully reachable during business hours.

•	Remote Team Experience: Comfortable working with a team you have never met in person. Proactive communication and follow-through are essential.



REQUIRED SOFTWARE PROFICIENCY

QuickBooks Online	       Expert Required	QuickBooks Online

Property Mgmt Software	Required	        DoorLoop (preferred)

STR / Guest Comms	       Required	                Hospitable (preferred)

Construction PM Software	Required	JobTread (preferred)

Gmail & Google Workspace	Required	Google Workspace



WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE IN 90 DAYS

•	Books are current, reconciled, and clean with no open questions.

•	Payroll runs on time every cycle without reminders from ownership.

•	All
s and calls are answered within 48 business hours.

•	Project managers, field team and sales feel fully supported — no paperwork falling behind.

•	Tenants and guests receive timely, professional responses.

•	Ownership is focused on growth, not chasing operations.



HOW TO APPLY

Please submit the following to be considered for this position:

•	Your updated resume clearly listing relevant software experience.

•	A short video (2 minutes or less) introducing yourself and walking through your QuickBooks Online experience.

•	In your application or resume, list every property management, short-term rental, and construction project management platform you have worked in.



Candidates who do not submit a video introduction will not be considered. This step is required and is our first screen for communication quality. 



Thank you!

title: Short-Term Rental Operations Specialist — Virtual Assistant
description:
We operate a portfolio of short-term rental properties and services across the UK under three brands - AStar Stays, The Noir Collective, and Property Cartel. We care deeply about the guest experience, the quality of our properties, and the reputation we're building.



We're building an AI-driven operation and we move fast - we want people around us who are excited by that, not intimidated by it. We're growing, and we need someone who can grow with us.



The role

This is a remote VA position at the heart of our operations - you'll be running the day-to-day across a live portfolio of properties, owning the guest experience end-to-end, and making real decisions that directly affect our reputation and revenue.



You'll be the first point of contact for guests, the person who spots problems before they become complaints, and the operator who keeps things moving when the directors aren't in the room. This role carries genuine weight. We're not looking for someone to assist - we're looking for someone to own.



What you'll actually be doing:



Managing guest communications across WhatsApp Business, Airbnb, and 

Running pre-check-in readiness checks and coordinating with cleaners

Handling maintenance issues, guest complaints, and last-minute problems - often outside of office hours

Responding to reviews and protecting our ratings

Monitoring our pricing software for pricing anomalies

Managing tasks in our project management software and flagging anything that needs director attention

Identifying patterns and suggesting operational improvements

What we're looking for

You don't need a background in short-term rentals - though it helps. What you do need is:



Excellent written communication. Your messages to guests need to be warm, clear, and professional. Not corporate. Not templated.

Strong judgement. You'll be making decisions independently. We need to trust that you'll make the right call.

Genuine attention to detail. The kind that notices when something is slightly off and does something about it.

Calm under pressure. Things go wrong in this industry - at 11pm on a Friday. We need someone who handles that without panicking.

Reliable availability. You'll need to be responsive during agreed hours and reachable for genuine out-of-hours emergencies.

Comfortable working alongside AI tools - we use them across the business and expect our team to embrace them.

The hiring process

We run a structured, multi-stage process. We do this because the role matters and we take hiring seriously. If you're not prepared to invest time in the process, this probably isn't the right fit.



1. Written application - CV and written questions

2. Video submission - a short video introduction

3. Skills assessment - three short video-based tests

4. Two interviews - one with each director

5. Trial - a day of real work tasks



We communicate clearly at every stage and we don't leave people waiting without reason.



What we offer

Fully remote

Competitive salary - discussed at interview stage based on experience

A role with real responsibility from Day 1

A small, direct team - you'll work closely with both directors

Room to grow as the portfolio grows



How to apply

Send your CV and answers to the following questions to 
 you write anything else, begin your
 with the phrase: the detail is in the brief.



1. When everything lands at once - messages, a cleaner issue, a guest complaint, an overdue task - how do you d
 what gets your attention first? Answer in no more than five sentences.

2. What does doing this job well look like to you?

3. Why this role with us - not just this type of role generally?



Once you've submitted, all correspondence will come from 
 - please add this to your contacts or check your spam folder so you don't miss anything.

title: Inventory & Forecasting Specialist – Optimize Stock & Profitability
description:
Are you driven by numbers, data, and fast-paced decision-making? Do you want to be responsible for ensuring we always have the right products in stock at the right price?



At TEESHOPPEN / It's May, we are looking for an analytically strong Inventory Specialist to take full ownership of our stock balance. Your mission is to ensure a healthy inventory flow, maximize profit, and minimize deadstock.



Your Primary Responsibilities:

- Deadstock Monitoring: Identify slow-moving items and suggest immediate actions (e.g., sales, campaigns, or bundles) to free up capital.

- PeakWMS (Warehouse Management System)

- Prediko (AI-powered forecasting tool)

- Google Sheets (For advanced data analysis)



Who Are You?

- Analytical Mindset: You love numbers, but you understand the commercial reality behind them. You don't just see a stock list; you see opportunities to increase the bottom line.

- Proactive & Self-Driven: You don’t wait for instructions. You spot inventory imbalances or profit opportunities before anyone else.

- Tech-Savvy: You quickly master new software and understand how data flows between WMS and e-commerce platforms.

- Responsible: You understand that a healthy inventory balance is the heartbeat of our business and cash flow.



What We Offer:

- A long-term position with significant responsibility.

- The chance to work with cutting-edge AI forecasting tools.

- A role where your decisions directly impact the company's profitability.

- How to Apply: Please describe your previous experience with procurement, inventory management, or data analysis. If you have experience with our specific tech stack (Shopify, PeakWMS, Prediko), it is a huge plus.

title: Dental Billing and Dental Insurance Collection DR26-0401
description:
Dental Billing Specialist Position



The Dental Billing Specialist is responsible for the accurate and timely preparation, submission, and management of dental insurance claims in alignment with payer requirements and industry standards. This role requires a strong understanding of dental coding, reimbursement methodologies, and revenue cycle workflows to ensure optimal claim acceptance and maximum reimbursement. 



Work Schedule Requirement:

This position is structured to support U.S.-based operations and therefore requires availability to work during morning hours in the Philippines, aligned with U.S. business hours at night.



The Specialist will perform end-to-end billing functions only, verification of proper assignment of CDT codes, and submission of claims through clearinghouses and payer portals. 



Candidates must demonstrate the ability to interpret Explanation of Benefits (EOBs), reconcile payments accurately within the practice management system (such as Dentrix, Open Dental, or Eaglesoft), and ensure alignment between billed services, contractual allowances, and collected amounts. Strong analytical skills are required to identify underpayments, trends in denials, and opportunities for revenue optimization.



This position operates within a highly structured and performance-driven environment, requiring strict adherence to internal controls, compliance standards, and productivity benchmarks. Attention to detail, accountability, and the ability to work independently while maintaining high accuracy are critical for success.



Requirements:



Minimum of 1–2 years of proven experience in dental billing and insurance processing

Strong knowledge of CDT coding, dental procedures, and insurance terminology

Experience with claim submission, denial management, and payment posting

Proficiency in dental practice management software (Dentrix, Open Dental, Eaglesoft, or similar)

Ability to analyze EOBs and resolve discrepancies

Must successfully pass a technical assessment evaluating billing accuracy and knowledge

Dental Insurance Collector Position



The Dental Insurance Collector is responsible for the proactive follow-up and resolution of outstanding insurance claims to ensure timely reimbursement and minimize accounts receivable aging. This role focuses on payer communication, denial resolution, and recovery of underpaid or delayed claims.



The Collector will conduct systematic follow-ups with insurance companies via phone, payer portals, and written correspondence to determine claim status, resolve processing issues, and escalate complex cases when necessary. This includes analyzing denial codes, identifying root causes, and preparing and submitting appeals with appropriate documentation.



Candidates must demonstrate strong negotiation and communication skills, as well as the ability to maintain persistence and professionalism when interacting with insurance representatives. The role requires a deep understanding of payer policies, coordination of benefits, frequency limitations, and contractual obligations to effectively advocate for proper reimbursement.



The position also involves maintaining detailed and accurate documentation of all follow-up activities, updating claim statuses in the practice management system, and contributing to monthly reporting metrics related to collection performance and denial trends.



This is a performance-based role within a high-volume environment, requiring efficiency, consistency, and the ability to meet defined productivity and recovery targets.



Requirements:



Minimum of 1–2 years of experience in dental insurance collections or accounts receivable follow-up

Strong understanding of the insurance claim lifecycle and denial management

Experience communicating with insurance carriers and navigating payer portals

Ability to read and interpret EOBs and identify underpayments

Strong organizational and documentation skills

Must successfully pass a competency test focused on claim follow-up and denial resolution

title: Bookkeeper
description:
Join Our Remote Accounting Team!

We are a fast-growing, boutique outsourced accounting firm supporting high-growth e-commerce and service-based businesses. While our name remains confidential for now, we’ve been behind the scenes helping some of the most exciting DTC brands scale with confidence through expert bookkeeping, financial systems, and strategic insights.



We are a remote-first company with a collaborative, client-first culture — and we’re looking for exceptional talent to grow with us.



We’re seeking a detail-oriented, tech-savvy E-commerce Bookkeeper to manage financial records, process transactions, and generate critical reports to support client decision-making. You’ll play a key role in ensuring books are accurate, clean, and up-to-date — and bring clarity to fast-moving businesses.



What You’ll Be Doing

As our Bookkeeper, you’ll support multiple clients with day-to-day accounting operations, reconciling accounts, and managing both AP and AR functions.



Your Core Responsibilities:

- Record and categorize financial transactions using QuickBooks Online

- Reconcile bank, credit card, and merchant accounts

- Prepare and analyze financial reports (P&L, Balance Sheets, Cash Flow Statements)

- Perform invoicing, collections, and accounts receivable tracking

- Process bills and manage accounts payable workflows

- Process payroll and ensure timely tax remittances

- Track and maintain accurate inventory accounting records

- Communicate with clients via dedicated channels (Slack,
, Meetings)

- Support ad hoc bookkeeping and administrative needs as required



What You’ll Bring to the Table

**Experience & Skills:

- 3+ years of experience as a Bookkeeper

- Advanced QuickBooks Online skills are required

- Strong grasp of inventory tracking and accounting best practices

- Experience reconciling financial data across multiple platforms

- Experience with NetSuite is required

- Proficient with Google Workspace, Excel, and Word

- Experience working with e-commerce or service-based businesses is a plus

**Communication & Work Environment:

- Advanced English skills (written and spoken – C1 level preferred)

- Strong attention to detail and time management

- Clear communicator who can work independently and manage deadlines

- Tech-savvy and comfortable with remote collaboration tools



What’s In It For You?

- 100% Remote Work - Work from anywhere in the Philippines. No more long commutes or stressful traffic. Spend more quality time with your loved ones and thrive in the comfort of your own home.

- Long-Term Career Growth – With opportunities to scale into higher leadership roles.

Competitive Pay – $10/hr + benefits as an independent contractor

- Supportive Culture – Work alongside a leadership team that values transparency, innovation, and your ideas.

- Positive, People-First Vibes – We believe in balance, fun, and thriving together.

- Annual Performance Reviews – Your growth gets recognized and rewarded.



Ready to Keep the Books Clean & Clients Confident?

If you’re obsessed with accuracy, enjoy structure, and love bringing order to financial chaos — this role is for you. Join our growing team of professionals who value excellence, clarity, and results.

title: SALES CONSULTANT — Phone Closer + DM Closer
description:
You must speak and write fluent, clear English to be considered.

This role uses a time tracker and screen monitor. If you are not okay with this, please do not apply.

 

---

 

ABOUT THE ROLE

 

We are a U.S.-based credit repair and capital access company. We help entrepreneurs fix their credit and get the business funding they need to grow.

 

We are hiring one Sales Consultant to handle two things:

1. Phone consultations — closing new clients on our credit repair program

2. DM closing — moving warm leads from interested to enrolled through Instagram and
 messages

 

We will train you on the credit piece. You bring the sales skills and the hunger.

 

This is a part-time role to start (20–25 hours/week). As our client volume grows, so do your hours. The right person will grow into a full-time role here.

 

---

 

WHAT YOU WILL DO

 

- Run credit consultations over the phone and close clients on our program

- Close warm leads through 
 on Instagram and

- Follow up with new leads within 5 minutes — no exceptions

- Log every enrolled client into Go High Level (GHL) same day

- Track your pipeline, consultations, and activity in GHL

- Report weekly on close rate and revenue closed

 

---

 

PAY STRUCTURE

 

Hourly Base: $5.00 USD/hour (paid based on tracked hours)

Part-time start: 20–25 hours/week = approx. $400–$500/month base

 

Commission:

- $25 per monthly enrollment (client pays $250/mo)

- $100 per pay-in-full enrollment (client pays $1,500 upfront)

 

Performance Bonus:

- Close $3,000+ in enrolled revenue in a month = extra $150 bonus

 

Example earnings at 25 hrs/week:

$500 base + 8 monthly closes ($200) + 3 pay-in-full closes ($300) + bonus ($150) = $1,150/month

 

The more you close, the more you earn. Commission is uncapped.

 

---

 

REQUIREMENTS

 

Must have:

- Fluent English — spoken and written (non-negotiable)

- 1–2+ years of phone sales or DM closing experience

- Strong ability to connect with people and guide them to a decision

- Available during U.S. business hours (EST)

- Reliable internet and quiet workspace for calls

- Comfortable with time tracking and screen monitoring

 

Nice to have:

- Experience with Go High Level (GHL)

- Background in financial services, credit, or consulting

- DM sales experience on Instagram or

- Experience selling to entrepreneurs or small business owners

 

---

 

YOU ARE THE RIGHT FIT IF...

 

You are hungry, coachable, and ready to execute every day

You understand that people buy with emotion and justify with logic

You can hear what a client is NOT saying and meet them there

You have no problem with accountability — trackers, metrics, weekly check-ins

You want to grow into a full-time role with a company that rewards performance

 

---

 

HOW TO APPLY

 

Send the following:

1. Your resume or a brief summary of your sales background

2. Your location and time zone

3. A 60–90 second voice memo or video — tell us who you are, your best sales result (what you sold, at what price, and your close rate), and why you are the right person for this role

 

We want to hear how you communicate before we get on a call.

 

---

 

THE COMPANY CONNECT | 

Hungry closers only. Apply with receipts.

description:

We are scaling aggressively — and we need a performance-driven Meta Ads Specialist who understands how to generate qualified leads at scale.



This is not a beginner role.

This is for someone who lives inside Meta Ads Manager.

TO APPLY
