title: Sales Closer — SaaS Product | US Market | Base + Commission
description:
This is not a cold calling job. This is not a setter role. This is a closing role.

Leads are generated for you. Demos are booked for you. Your job is to show up, run the demo, and get the signature. If you have done this before and you are good at it — read on.



About Leaddi:

Leaddi is a fast growing SaaS platform that helps home service businesses in the United States plumbers, HVAC companies, and garage door operators recover revenue they lose to missed calls. The product is live, the market is massive, and we are scaling fast.

The pain we solve is simple. The ROI is immediate. The demo sells itself if you can run it confidently.



What you will be doing:



Receiving warm leads and booked demo appointments from our calling team

Running 10 to 15 minute screen share demos showing exactly how Leaddi works

Handling price objections and closing deals on the spot

Following up with no-shows and warm prospects until a decision is made

Reporting pipeline status and feedback to the founder daily





You are the right person if:



You have at least 1 year of closing experience in a phone or video sales role

You have closed deals in the US market and understand how American business owners think

Your English is fluent, confident, and neutral enough that a Florida plumber would never question it

You can run a live product demo without reading from a script

You are comfortable asking for money and handling pushback without flinching

You are self managed and results driven — you do not need to be chased to perform





You are the wrong person if:



You have only done setting or customer support

You need heavy management or daily motivation

You have a strong accent that would create friction on calls with US business owners

You have never been measured on close rate or revenue generated

You need warm leads handed to you AND a script AND a manager watching over you to perform





Compensation:



Base salary: competitive, based on experience

$50 flat commission per closed deal, paid when the client pays

10% monthly residual on every active client you closed, every month they stay active

Uncapped — the more you close the more you earn permanently



What this looks like in real numbers:

Close 15 deals in month 1 — $750 upfront plus residuals starting to build.

By month 3 with 30 active clients — $594 per month in residuals alone before closing a single new deal.

By month 6 a consistently performing closer is earning $1,500 plus per month with most of it coming from clients already closed.

This is not a gig. This is a compounding income structure.



To apply you must do three things:

1. Answer these two questions in 3 sentences max each:



What is the best deal you have ever closed and why are you proud of it?

A prospect says "I need to think about it" — what do you say word for word?



2. Record a 2 minute mock closing call. You are on a screen share with Mike, a plumber in Florida with a 3 truck operation. He has seen the demo. He says "it looks interesting but I need to think about it." Get him to say yes before the call ends. Do not use a script. Do not ask for permission. Just close.

3. Send both with your application.

No recording, no response. No answers to both questions, no response. Generic applications are deleted without reading.

We are growing fast. The right person starts this week.

title: WANTED! The World’s Most Reliable Operations Project Manager (Integrator /Finisher)
description:
Who We Are:

We are a fast-moving entrepreneurial group running multiple companies. Ideas come fast, projects hit 80% completion quickly, and chaos is constant across marketing, operations, and growth initiatives. We need a grounded anchor who thrives on turning that chaos into order across several businesses at once.



About Me (The Visionary): 

I am a fast-paced entrepreneur and "Creator." My mind is constantly buzzing with new business ideas, questions, and insights. Because of this, I am exceptionally good at starting projects, innovating, and solving complex big-picture problems.

However, I am terrible at finishing them. I have virtually zero patience for routine, and my eyes glaze over at humdrum administrative tasks. I move fast, communicate very directly, prioritize logic over emotion, and I am currently sitting on a mountain of 80%-completed ideas that need to be brought to the marketplace.



What We’re Looking For:

We are looking for the ultimate Integrator / Operations Project Manager — the grounded anchor who takes 80%-done ideas across multiple companies, builds strict timelines, manages all day-to-day administrative and marketing operations chaos, and ruthlessly pushes every project across the finish line so the visionary can stay 100% focused on innovation.



Key Responsibilities (you will own these completely):

- Turn raw, messy ideas into clear, prioritized spreadsheets with timelines, budgets, metrics, and owners — across multiple companies

- Build and manage strict project timelines for both operations and marketing initiatives

- Handle all administrative “humdrum” tasks (software, spreadsheets, daily ops, marketing execution) independently

- Shield the visionary from chaos and proactively push back when new ideas risk derailing current priorities

- Track data, metrics, and historical facts to drive every decision — and actively leverage AI tools to 10x speed and output



Your Core Responsibilities:

The Finisher: 

Take my raw, 80%-completed ideas, build a timeline, and ruthlessly execute the daily details to take them to 100%.



The Administrative Shield: 

Take complete ownership of the day-to-day operations, software setups, spreadsheets, and scheduling so I can focus exclusively on the creative process and innovation.



The Reality Check: 

Act as my anchor. You must have the confidence to push back, tell me "No," and force me to slow down and figure out how to implement my current idea before allowing me to jump to the next shiny object



Qualifications:

We care far more about who you are than where you went to school or how many years you’ve been titled “manager.” If you live and breathe the traits below, we want to talk to you.

You are well-versed in modern AI tools and know how to wield them to actually get results — not just chat or play with them. You use AI daily to amplify your own execution, automate repetitive tasks, analyze data faster, and multiply your output across operations and marketing.



Salary & Growth

50,000 – 100,000 Per month to start depending on experience, AI implementation expertise and proven results.

Top performers have the ability to move up and earn more per month within 12–18 months as the companies scale.



Personality Profile – This Should Be You

- Wealth Dynamics: Accumulator (primary) or Mechanic

- 16Personalities (MBTI): ISTJ (Logistician) or ESTJ (Executive)

- DISC: High Steadiness (S) + High Compliance (C) — the exact opposite of the visionary you’ll support



You love routine, repetitive execution. You become obsessed with tying up loose ends at 80% to reach 100%. You are data-driven, patient, reliable, and take pride in rules, spreadsheets, and accuracy. You have the confidence to tell the boss “No” when needed. You treat AI as a powerful multiplier, not a toy.



The Personality Traits You Must Have:



1) High Steadiness & Follow-Through: 

You actually like routine. You are steady, dependable, and take deep pride in ensuring absolutely nothing falls through the cracks.



2) Process over Possibility: 

You prefer concrete data, metrics, and checklists over abstract brainstorming.



3) Thick Skin & Logical Communication: 

I am a highly logical, direct communicator who is focused on objective truth and results. I do not play games, I do not filter my opinions, and I expect you to be just as frank with me. You must be someone who does not require constant emotional validation or cheerleading to do a great job.

title: Elite Google Ads & LSA Media Buyer, Home Services Agency (Full-Time | $1,500/mo + Growth)
description:
ABOUT US - CONTRACTOR KINGDOM

 

We're a US-based marketing agency that works exclusively with contractors: HVAC, plumbing, asphalt, and more. We build their websites, run their local SEO, and manage their paid ads. Our clients are tradespeople who are great at their craft but need a team behind them to keep the phones ringing.

 

We're growing very fast. Our goal is to be the best contractor marketing agency in the industry, and we're building the team to get there.

 

For the right person, this role grows as the company grows. Your pay, your scope, your title, all of it scales with the results you put on the board. We're not looking for someone who wants a job. We're looking for someone who wants to build a career here.

 

 

WHAT WE'RE ACTUALLY LOOKING FOR

 

The best google media buyer we can find. Simple as that.

 

Not someone who's "pretty good at Google Ads." Not someone who can follow a checklist. We want the person who lives and breathes this stuff. Someone who can look at a campaign and know what's broken, who has strong opinions on ad group structure, who's confident running a $1,000/mo account and a $10k/mo account with equal precision & strategy to adapt to ad spend.

 

We currently manage 14 Local Service Ads accounts and 4 Google Ads (Search/PMax) campaigns, with more coming every month. You'll own all of it. As our Google Ads volume grows, you'll shift toward full-time CPC strategy while we bring someone in underneath you for LSA. This is a growth seat.

 

 

THE ROLE

 

You own campaign performance end-to-end. When a cost-per-lead spikes, you're already in the data figuring out why. When an LSA insurance doc is 30 days from expiring, you've already flagged it. When a Google Ads campaign has been running the same structure for months, you're not waiting to be told to optimize it.

 

LSA (14 accounts, growing):

For new accounts, set up bios, photos, hours matching GBP exactly, service areas by county/zip, and handle any verification errors.

For existing accounts, proactively track and manage insurance, licenses, and background check statuses. Rate leads with detailed notes. Listen to call recordings to confirm owners are answering the phone. Flag problems before they become fires.

 

Google Ads (4 accounts, growing fast):

Remodel/ Optimize our current Search and PMax campaigns for home service contractors with budgets from modest to aggressive. Analyze performance data, identify waste, and make strategic adjustments. Be able to articulate clearly WHY you've structured an account the way you have, especially for smaller-budget clients where every dollar matters and strategy is everything in competitive markets.

 

You are NOT responsible for creating GA accounts, running onboarding calls, or billing setup. That's handled before accounts reach you.

 

 

ON AI

 

We use Claude to analyze campaign data, build internal tools, and make our team more efficient. All AI subscriptions are paid for. You don't need to come in with AI workflows already built. We can package your knowledge into SOPs into prompts and AI systems for you to make your role efficient & most effective.  

 

What matters more is that you have the underlying knowledge and strategy. A great media buyer using AI beats an average one using AI every single time.

 

 

WHO YOU ARE

 

You've managed Local Service Ads AND Google Ads campaigns, ideally in a multi-account agency environment. You can build a Search campaign from scratch, structure ad groups with intent, and explain your bidding strategy in plain English. You understand LSA verification workflows and lead credit systems.

 

More than anything, you're scrappy and you care. You take results personally. You don't coast on autopilot campaigns. You treat every client's ad budget like it's coming out of your own pocket.

 

Required:

- 3+ years hands-on experience with Google Local Service Ads AND Google Ads (Search, PMax)

- Proven track record of driving results, not just managing accounts

- Excellent written and spoken English

- Comfortable recording clear, professional Loom video updates

- Organized enough to run 18+ accounts without anything slipping through cracks

- Proactive by nature

- Competitive 

- Reliable internet and a quiet, professional remote workspace

 

Bonus:

- Meta/Facebook Ads experience

- Home service business experience (HVAC, plumbing, roofing, etc.)

- Call tracking platform experience (WhatConverts, CallRail)

- Google Ads certification  

- Already using AI in your workflow (not required, but a plus)

- Leadership Skills

 

COMPENSATION

 

$1,500/month USD starting salary. Performance-based raises. Full-time, flexible hours, but you need to be available during US business hours for client communication. As CK results & managed accounts grow, your role and pay grow with it.

 

 

HOW TO APPLY

 

Start your application with the words "ADS READY" so we know you read this.

 

Then three things:

 

1. Show us results. Pick one Google Ads account you've managed (preferably US based home service/blue collar) . 

Tell us the starting point, what you did, and where it ended up, with real numbers (CPL, conversion rate, spend, leads, etc.). 

 

2. How would you approach a home services client with a $1,500/mo Google Ads budget? What's your strategy, and what would you do differently than with a $15,000/mo ad spend client?

 

3. Bonus: Send us a loom of the above 2 questions and why you think you would thrive in this role? 

 

The strongest candidates will move to a paid audit round where we'll give you access to one of our real accounts and ask you what you'd change. We pay for your time on that.

description:

Your reference point 
 on TikTok and Instagram. If you don't know who that is, look him up before you apply. That style, intentional pacing, B-roll layering, subtitles that feel like part of the story, is the standard we're building toward.

This is not a side gig. This is not a creative-on-your-own-schedule role. If you're not available Monday through Friday, 9am to 5pm MST, don't apply.

Hours

 and the personal accounts of Liam Benson and Lena Oeun. You'll work closely with both. Know who you're applying to work with.





WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

Nearly fluent English, written and spoken.

Available Mon to Fri, 9am to 5pm MST. US hours, non-negotiable.

 with

title: Client Service Specialist - Associate
description:
The Client Service Specialist – Associate role supports the stability, performance, and day-to-day operation of hosted application and infrastructure environments. This includes monitoring system health, responding to i
, and executing operational processes across cloud and application layers.



The role combines system administration, production support, and DevOps practices, with a focus on proactive monitoring, i
 response, and continuous improvement of system reliability. Responsibilities include troubleshooting across infrastructure and applications, supporting deployments and maintenance, and ensuring system availability and performance. The role partners with engineering, product operations, and client-facing teams to resolve issues and validate system changes, and requires familiarity with cloud platforms (e.g., Amazon Web Services) and observability tools (e.g., Datadog, Grafana, ELK), with an expectation of independent operational ownership, on-call participation, and contributions to automation and process improvement.



Responsibilities: 

• Administer the PCI application suite within hosted (cloud-based) environments, monitoring application and infrastructure health using tools such as Datadog, Grafana, and ELK (Kibana), and responding to alerts related to performance, capacity, and availability

• Triage and resolve i
 across production and lower environments, including monitoring support channels/calls and prioritizing incoming requests; leverage knowledge of the PCI application suite and hosted environments to diagnose issues, guide resolution, and determine appropriate escalation

• Act as a primary point of contact for operational issues and requests, coordinating across engineering, product operations, and client-facing teams to drive resolution, support system changes, and validate system performance following deployments or maintenance

• Execute routine operational tasks to maintain system stability, including deployments, patching, failover activities, and environment refresh processes (e.g., DR/HA, gold copies)

• Support cloud-based environments in Amazon Web Services (AWS), including compute, storage, and networking components, as well as application servers and database systems (e.g., Oracle)

• Follow and contribute to operational runbooks and standard procedures to ensure consistency and reliability in system operations

• Identify opportunities to improve efficiency by reducing manual processes and contributing to automation, monitoring enhancements, and alert tuning

• Manage and update work through ticketing systems (e.g., JIRA, ServiceNow), ensuring clear documentation and adherence to SLAs



Skills and Qualifications:  

• 0–3 years of experience in technical operations, system administration, DevOps, or related roles

• Foundational knowledge of cloud platforms, preferably Amazon Web Services (AWS)

• (AWS Cloud Practitioner or Solutions Architect Associate certification is a plus)

• Understanding of core infrastructure concepts, including:

• Compute, networking, and storage

• Linux and/or Windows system administration

• Basic scripting (e.g., Python, Bash, or PowerShell)

• Exposure to or interest in site reliability and operational best practices, including monitoring, alerting, and i
 response

• Ability to troubleshoot technical issues across systems, logs, and environments using a structured, analytical approach

• Familiarity with observability and monitoring tools such as:

• Datadog

• Grafana

• ELK stack (Kibana)

• Strong problem-solving mindset with a focus on automation, efficiency, and reducing manual processes

• Ability to manage multiple tasks, i
, and priorities in a fast-paced operational environment

• Strong communication skills, including the ability to translate technical issues for non-technical stakeholders

• Experience with ticketing systems (e.g., JIRA, ServiceNow) and structured operational workflows is a plus

• Exposure to databases (e.g., Oracle) or enterprise application environments is a plus

• Prior experience in a client-facing or support role is a plus, particularly when combined with technical troubleshooting responsibilities

IMPORTANTE: availability for a 4-day, 10-hour shift (Friday to Monday): This is a permanent requirement for the foreseeable future.

title: Video Editor
description:
ABOUT US

Fullmer Consulting Media is the content team behind Logan Fullmer — a real estate investor and entrepreneur with 290,000+ followers across Instagram, YouTube, and
. We produce high-volume, formula-driven content: property tours, deal breakdowns, AI-powered reels, podcast clips, and entrepreneur content.



We run a structured production system. Every format has a defined template, every edit starts with a clear brief, and every editor receives specific feedback. We're scaling fast and hiring 2–3 reliable overseas editors to grow with us.



---



WHAT YOU'LL DO

- Edit short-form content (Instagram Reels, TikTok,
) in Adobe Premiere Pro and CapCut following provided templates and shot-by-shot briefs

- Edit long-form content (YouTube videos, podcast episodes) per format guides

- Apply hooks, pacing cuts, captions, lower thirds, and safe zones exactly as briefed

- Deliver files formatted and labeled per platform specs (aspect ratios, file naming, caption burn-ins)

- Submit edits within agreed turnaround windows (typically 24–48 hours)

- Accept feedback clearly and apply revisions quickly



---



REQUIREMENTS

- 2+ years of video editing experience (short-form social and/or long-form YouTube/podcast)

- Proficient in Adobe Premiere Pro (required) and CapCut (required)

- Strong English reading comprehension — briefs and feedback are in English

- Reliable internet and ability to send/receive large video files

- Comfortable working remotely and asynchronously without constant supervision

- Able to follow a template or style guide precisely and consistently



NICE TO HAVE

- Experience with real estate or business/entrepreneur content

- Familiarity with AI video tools (Kling, Veo 3, HeyGen, ElevenLabs)

- Experience clipping short-form content from long-form interviews or podcasts



---



WHY THIS ROLE

- Every project comes with a detailed brief and reference edits — no guessing what "good" means

- Consistent volume every month — reliable editors move from project-based to retainer

- Specific, actionable feedback — we invest in making editors better over time

- Clear expectations, stable workflows, and a structured global team



---



HOW TO APPLY

Please send the following:



1. Portfolio or reel link — must include at least 5 examples of short-form social content (Reels, TikTok, or YouTube Shorts) and 1 example of long-form content

2. Brief intro (3–5 sentences): who you are, where you're located, your experience, and your availability

3. List of tools you use regularly

4. Your typical turnaround time for a 60–90 second Reel edit from raw footage



Shortlisted candidates will receive a paid test week before any offer is made.

- Do it again tomorrow.

If you enjoy high?volume outbound, talking to small business owners, and closing on the first call, you’ll like this.



What You Will Do

- Call 100+ new smoke shop leads per day using our lists and scripts

- Make 7–14 touches per lead (calls / SMS /
) until you:

- Reach the decision maker and get a “yes” or “no,” or

- Get a usable
 to add to our nurture list

- Run the full sales cycle on the phone:

- Get past the gatekeeper

- Qualify the owner in under 60 seconds

- Present our offer, including free samples and first test order

- Handle objections and close on the line

- Log every touch and outcome in our CRM

- Follow up on interested but und
 shops until they buy or clearly decline



Scoreboard & Daily Targets

We keep it simple and transparent. You will see your numbers every day.



Daily Activity Targets

- 100+ new outbound calls

- 40–60 follow?up calls / texts /
s

- All leads touched at least once every 2–3 days until 7–14 touches



Weekly Outcome Targets

- 20+ decision?maker conversations

- At least 2% close rate on new leads (goal: 2–4 new stores per week once ramped)

- New decision?maker
s captured and added to nurture sequence

We will train you on the product and scripts, but hitting these numbers consistently is non?negotiable.



What Success Looks Like After 90 Days

- You are closing 2%+ of new leads into first orders or committed sample trials

- You consistently close 8–15 new stores per month from your own pipeline

- Your notes are clean, every lead is followed up properly, and nothing falls through the cracks



Requirements

- Strong spoken English, confident and clear on the phone with U.S. business owners

- Proven experience in outbound phone sales or cold calling (B2B preferred)

- Comfortable doing high?volume dials every day

- Can follow a script and a process, not “wing it” after a week

- Reliable internet, quiet workspace, and ability to work U.S. time zones



Compensation

Base salary: $400–800 USD / month (depending on experience and schedule)

Commission: 10% of revenue from the first order for every store you close

There is no cap on commissions. The more stores you open, the more you earn.



How to Apply

Send a short message with:

1) Your outbound sales experience

2) Your internet speed and location

3) Record a 1–2 minute audio or video reading a sample cold?call intro (we’ll send the script).

If it sounds good, we’ll invite you to a group interview and then a paid test shift calling real smoke shops.

We don’t care about fancy resumes. We care that you show up, do the calls, and close.

title: Senior Bookkeeper
description:
Join Gelt: Transforming the Tax Experience



At Gelt, we're revolutionizing the tax industry with our premium tax solutions powered by advanced technology and expert CPAs. Our mission is to provide personalized and proactive tax strategies that help our clients maximize their financial potential and keep more of their hard-earned money.



Visit us at 
.



What We Are Looking For:



We are looking for a Senior Bookkeeper who will mainly focus on Gelt's internal finance operations.



Are you a financial expert with a passion for accuracy and a deep understanding of US laws, accounting, and taxes? Do you excel in dynamic, international settings and enjoy collaborating with US clients?



In addition to your expertise, we seek someone who is professional and has excellent English communication skills, both written and verbal. The ideal candidate should be dynamic and results-driven. If this describes you, we have the perfect opportunity waiting!



In this role, you will:



- Monitor accounts payable and process payment batches.

- Process US payroll and manage employee onboarding and offboarding.

- Prepare and manage the audit and company's US tax returns and compliance.

- Record Financial Transactions: Maintain accurate records of all income, expenses, and journal entries in accounting software (QuickBooks)

- Bank & Credit Card Reconciliation: Reconcile monthly statements to ensure all transactions are properly categorized and matched.

- Expense Tracking: Monitor and categorize business expenses to ensure proper cost allocation.

- Payroll Support: Prepare and process payroll or assist with payroll entries and related tax filings.

- Oversee and manage all aspects of the company’s financial and accounting functions.

- Ensure compliance with US GAAP and other regulatory requirements.

- Comply with reporting requirements using knowledge of US local laws.

- Prepare and review consolidated monthly, quarterly, and annual financial statements and reports.

- Consolidate US financial statements and overall financial statements, and analyze the budget versus actual performance.

- Financial Reporting: Generate financial statements (Profit & Loss, Balance Sheet, Cash Flow).



About You:

Core Qualifications:



- Accounting licenses or certifications, such as Certified Public Accountant (CPA) or Chartered Accountant (CA)

- Proficiency with ERP systems (Microsoft Business Central), QuickBooks Online, Google Sheets, and system integrations.

- Expertise in US payroll is a significant advantage.

- High level of accuracy and attention to detail

- Strong problem-solving skills and the ability to address challenges independently

- Excellent verbal and written communication skills

- Minimum of 7 years of experience in accounting or finance, with at least 2 years in a controller or similar role

- In-depth knowledge of US GAAP and financial regulations

- Experience working with US clients is highly desirable

Proficiency in accounting software and Microsoft Office Suite, especially Excel

Technical Requirements (Device):



Laptop/Computer (at least 8GB RAM)

- Headset (preferably with a noise-canceling microphone)

- Fast/Stable Internet Connection (at least 10 Mbps)

- Available to work long-term within U.S. business hours

title: Outbound Sales (Call Transfer Specialist)
description:
Job Title: Outbound Call Specialist 

Location: Remote (Work From Home)

Employment Type:  Full-Time / Contract (flexible)



About the Role

We're a growing web design and SEO company helping small businesses build their online presence — and we need a motivated phone person to help us connect with more of them. This is not a high-pressure sales role. Your job is simple: call small business owners, have a genuine conversation, gauge their interest in our services, and hand off warm leads to our closing team.



If you're a natural conversationalist who can build quick rapport over the phone, this role is for you.



What You'll Be Doing



Making outbound calls to small business owners and decision-makers from provided contact lists



Introducing our web design and SEO services in a friendly, low-pressure way



Asking the right questions to determine if the prospect is a potential fit



Qualifying interest and transferring warm calls directly to a senior rep or closing specialist



Logging call outcomes and notes in our CRM or tracking sheet



Following up with contacts as needed based on scheduling or availability





What We're Looking For



Comfortable and confident on the phone — you don't mind making a lot of calls

Clear communicator who can explain things simply without a script feeling like a script

Self-motivated and able to manage your own time working remotely

Reliable with a quiet workspace, stable internet, and a working phone setup

Basic computer skills for logging notes and following internal processes

Positive attitude and thick skin — rejections are part of the game and you bounce back fast





Bonus Points (Not Required)



Prior telemarketing, cold calling, or outbound sales experience

Background in sales — if you can close, that's a huge plus and opens the door for more responsibility and higher earnings

Familiarity with web design, SEO, or digital marketing concepts

Experience with CRMs or call tracking tools





Compensation



Base pay per hour or per shift + performance bonuses for qualified transfers

Additional commission opportunity available for those with sales experience



(Specific rates discussed during interview)



Why Work With Us



Fully remote — work from home on your schedule

No cold pitching complex tech — our service is straightforward and affordable ($59/month)

Supportive team environment with a clear, simple process

Room to grow into a higher-earning sales role if you have the skills





To Apply



Send a brief message about yourself and any relevant phone or sales experience. A short intro call will be part of the process — so get ready to show us what you've got.

title: Senior Procurement Specialist | Hiring ASAP
description:
Full-Time | Remote | Philippines | Reports to: Chief Operating Officer



About the role

We are a fast-growing pet products company selling direct-to-consumer and through retail channels. Our products include manufactured and finished goods sourced primarily from China, with additional vendors in the US.



This is a newly created role — the first dedicated procurement hire in our company. The successful candidate will consolidate purchasing currently spread across multiple people, establish supplier relationships, create spend visibility, and build the systems and discipline that will support our growth.



Key responsibilities

• Consolidate all purchasing activity into a single, managed process covering raw materials, components, and finished goods

• Own the full supplier lifecycle: identification, vetting, onboarding, negotiation, and ongoing relationship management

• Manage USD 10–14M in annual spend across the supplier base, primarily in China

• Negotiate pricing, lead times, payment terms, and volume contracts with existing and new suppliers

• Issue and track purchase orders end-to-end, from requisition to receipt

• Align purchasing with inventory targets using our WMS (Finale Inventory) and demand planning inputs

• Build and maintain an Approved Vendor List (AVL) and supplier scorecard

• Identify cost savings opportunities and report on spend vs. budget to leadership

• Coordinate with freight forwarders and manage import logistics for inbound shipments from Asia

• Proactively flag supply chain risks and develop contingency plans



Required qualifications:

• Minimum 5 years of experience in procurement, sourcing, or supply chain management

• Proven experience sourcing from Chinese factories or trading companies

• Strong written and spoken English communication skills

• Demonstrated experience building or significantly improving a purchasing process

• Ability to work independently in a fast-paced, low-structure environment

• Solid understanding of import logistics, Incoterms, and freight coordination

• Prior experience working for a US-based or global company with remote/async work expectations



Preferred qualifications:

• Experience with WMS or inventory management tools (Finale Inventory training provided)

• Background in consumer products, pet products, or e-commerce supply chains

• Familiarity with quality control processes for manufactured goods

• CPSM or CSCP certification

• Experience configuring an ERP or procurement module

• Mandarin language skills (written or spoken)



Work setup:

This is a fully remote, asynchronous-first position. Written communication is the default. You will document decisions, maintain purchasing status in shared trackers, and communicate proactively without relying on calls.



The Philippines time zone (UTC+8) aligns with Chinese business hours, enabling real-time supplier communication during your regular workday. You will have a defined spend authority threshold for routine purchases and will report directly to the COO with a weekly async check-in.



90-day success milestones:

• All active suppliers identified and documented in a centralized supplier register

• All purchasing activity consolidated under this role

• A standardized purchase order process established and consistently followed

• Full spend visibility by category delivered to leadership

• Top supplier contracts reviewed with renegotiation initiated on a minimum of three



How to apply:

Please submit your resume via Google Drive link to be considered for this position.

title: **  QuickBooks Online Bookkeeper (Experienced Only) **
description:
* IMPORTANT: No AI-Generated Applications *



-- We are not accepting AI-generated or copy-paste applications.

-- If your response is generic or templated, it will be ignored. Please answer in your own words.



** QuickBooks Online Bookkeeper (Experienced Only) ** 

U.S.-based bookkeeping firm seeking a skilled, detail-oriented bookkeeper with real hands-on experience.



This is not an entry-level or AP/AR-only role. You must understand full-cycle bookkeeping.



RESPONSIBILITIES

-  Categorize and review transactions in QuickBooks Online

-  Perform bank & credit card reconciliations

-  Identify and fix errors, duplicates, and misclassifications

-  Maintain clean, accurate books

-  Assist with basic AP/AR when needed

-  Generate P&L and Balance Sheet reports

-  Track your work and follow internal processes



REQUIREMENTS

-  Strong experience with QuickBooks Online (required)

-  Can independently reconcile accounts and clean up books

-  Solid understanding of basic accounting principles

-  High attention to detail (accuracy is critical)

-  Organized, reliable, and able to follow systems

-  Able to work with some U.S. time overlap



NOT A FIT IF YOU...

-  Only have AP/AR or data entry experience

-  Don’t know how to fully reconcile accounts

-  Need constant supervision



* SCREENING QUESTIONS *



Please answer the following in detail:



-  Walk through your exact process for reconciling a bank account in QuickBooks Online. Step-by-step.

-  You open a client’s books and see that the bank balance does not match QuickBooks. What do you do? Be specific.

-  How do you determine the correct category for transactions when it's unclear? Give an example.

-  Describe a time you had to clean up messy books. What was wrong and how did you fix it?

-  What reports do you review to confirm the books are accurate before closing a month?



We’re looking for someone who takes ownership and delivers clean books consistently.

title: Customer Service VA for Fashion eCommerce Stores
description:
Hello,



We run a growing eCommerce dropshipping business with multiple fashion stores across different countries. We are looking for a long-term Customer Support tea
mber to join us — there is real growth opportunity for people who consistently deliver great work and care about the customer experience.



This is a Customer Support role where you will follow a clear, documented process to keep our customers happy. It becomes very straightforward once you understand each step.



PLATFORMS YOU MUST KNOW:

- Shopify (eCommerce platform)

- Slack (team communication)

- DeepL (translation)



RESPONSIBILITIES:

- Respond to customer
s regarding refunds, tracking numbers, returns, and FAQs

- Handle customer complaints with warmth and professionalism

- Process a minimum of 20
s per hour while maintaining quality and accuracy

- Report on refunds and complaints

- Updating and communicating with our suplier through Slack

- Maintain our 12-24-hour reply ETA for all customer inquiries

- Contribute to our documentation and help improve our support processes

- Represent the brand consistently — accurate answers, no duplicates, on-topic replies every time



WHAT WE PROVIDE:

- Full video training

- Clear SOPs to follow

- Direct access to our Lead Customer Support for questions

- A supportive team environment



REQUIREMENTS:

- Fluent written and spoken English

- Minimum 2 years of experience in Customer Support

- Organized, motivated, and eager to learn

- Available for weekly team calls

- Available to start immediately

- Available for a minimum of 40 hours per week once full-time (we start with a 2-week trial, then move to full-time)

- Not currently working another full-time job



HOW TO APPLY:

To show me you've read the full post carefully, please do the following:

1. Start your application with the word: strawberry

2. Tell me about similar customer support experience you've had

3. Explain briefly why you think you're a good fit for this role



Applications that don't follow these steps will not be reviewed.



Looking forward to your proposal.



Thank you,

Taha

title: Remote Marketing Research & Strategy Assistant
description:
We’re Hiring: Remote Marketing Research & Strategy Assistant (Part-Time | 15–20 hrs/week)



Company Name: Storyteller Media Team





Position: Remote Marketing Research & Strategy Assistant

Location: 100% Remote

Start Date: ASAP



About Us



Storyteller Media Team helps service-based businesses generate more qualified leads and sales through strategic video content, paid ads, and marketing systems.



We work with businesses that want more than just “nice-looking content.” We focus on strategies that actually help them grow - including better offers, stronger ad angles, smarter lead forms, and more effective creative ideas.



We’re growing and looking for a highly detail-oriented Marketing Research & Strategy Assistant who can help us stay sharp on what’s working in the market.



If you love research, pattern recognition, marketing psychology, and spotting winning ideas before everyone else does - this role is for you.



About the Role:



This is a backend research and strategy support role inside a marketing company.



You will not be responsible for running ad campaigns directly. Instead, your job is to help our team make better strategic decisions by researching what is currently working in the market across different industries.



You’ll work closely with the founder to help identify:



strong ad angles

winning offers

useful lead form strategies

competitor patterns

trending content ideas

creative concepts we can test for client campaigns



This role is for someone who can do more than just collect random information. We need someone who can organize findings clearly, identify patterns, and turn research into actionable ideas.



Responsibilities:



Research top-performing ads across service-based industries using platforms like Meta Ads Library, Instagram,
, TikTok, YouTube, and competitor pages



Break down winning ads by:



hook

offer

CTA

format/style

messaging angle

why it may be working



Research high-converting offers by industry:



free consultation

free inspection

limited-time deals

financing offers

same-day service offers

seasonal promotions

and similar local business offers



Research and recommend lead form questions that balance:



conversion rate

lead quality

low friction vs qualification



Track social media and ad creative trends, including:



content styles

hook structures

editing styles

CTA trends

promotional angles

seasonal ideas



Research competitors and summarize:



what they are doing well

what offers they are pushing

what messaging they repeat

where the opportunities are



Compile organized weekly research reports in Google Docs and/or spreadsheets



Present findings in a clear way so our team can actually use the ideas



Must-Haves:



Strong English communication (written is especially important)



Strong research ability and attention to detail



Ability to summarize findings clearly and organize them in a useful format



Understanding of marketing fundamentals such as:



hooks

offers

direct response

lead generation

ad creatives

CTAs



Familiarity with platforms like:



 / Instagram

Meta Ads Library

TikTok

YouTube

Google Docs / Sheets



Ability to identify patterns instead of just dumping information



Reliable, proactive, and able to work independently



Preferred (Strong Plus):



Experience working in a marketing agency or with local/service-based businesses



Experience researching ads, offers, creative trends, or competitor strategies



Basic knowledge of paid ads, direct response marketing, or social media strategy



Experience supporting a founder, strategist, or media buying team



Understanding of home service or local business marketing



Please Do NOT Apply If:



You have no understanding of marketing, offers, or advertising psychology



You can gather information but struggle to explain what actually matters



Your English writing is not strong enough to present clear research summaries



You need constant supervision to complete simple tasks



You miss details often or submit messy, unorganized work



You are looking for a media buying role where you will manage campaigns directly



You are juggling too many other roles and won’t be consistent or responsive



What You Get:



Flexible remote schedule



Opportunity to grow into a longer-term strategic support role



Direct collaboration with founder



Hands-on exposure to real client marketing strategy across multiple industries



A fast-moving team focused on helping businesses grow



How to Apply:



Before we schedule interviews, I’d love to get a quick Loom video from you so I can understand how you think, communicate, and break down marketing ideas.



Please record a 3–5 minute Loom answering the questions below:



Tell me about yourself and your experience with marketing research, ad research, or strategy support.

What experience do you have researching winning ads, offers, or competitors?

What platforms or tools do you use to find strong ad ideas and trends?

If I asked you to research the best ad angles and offers for a roofing company, how would you approach it?

How would you organize your findings so a marketing team could actually use them?

Why do you want to work with Storyteller Media Team, and why do you think you’d be a good fit?



Send your application to:







Please include:



Your Loom link

Your resume / portfolio if available

Your timezone

Your weekly availability

A short note on your experience with ad research, competitor research, or marketing strategy support



If it looks like a fit, we’ll reach out to schedule an interview.



Looking forward to seeing your video.

title: Cleaning Business Sales and Operations
description:
You know what it's like to be great at sales but stuck in a role where nobody notices. Where you close deals and nothing changes. That's not this job.



We're a residential cleaning company running three markets across the US. We need someone who can sell AND operate. Close the deal, then make sure the job actually happens.



YOU MUST HAVE PRIOR EXPERIENCE IN A CLEANING BUSINESS.



Let me be honest about this role. Half of it is sales. The other half is operations: managing a team of independent cleaners who call out sick, run late, miss areas, and need to be rescheduled. Clients get frustrated. Cleaners have bad days. You're the person in the middle making sure nobody falls through the cracks.



If you only want to sell, this isn't the role. If chaos stresses you out, this isn't the role. But if you're the kind of person who handles problems calmly, keeps ten things moving at once, and still picks up the phone with energy — keep reading.



Your schedule:



Saturday & Sunday: 12-hour shifts (US EDT)

Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday: 9:00am – 2:30pm EDT



This is a weekend-heavy role. If that doesn't work for your life, this isn't the right fit, and that's completely fine.



The sales side:



Inbound leads from Google Ads and local service ads. Speed matters, every minute counts

Follow-up sequences with leads who didn't book the first time. You don't give up after one attempt

Lead tracking in our CRM — every call logged, every outcome recorded, no exceptions



Please note that we are an automation and AI-heavy business. Most of the work comes to you in Slack. We try to make this as easy as possible for everyone involved. 



The operations side:



Daily scheduling: assigning cleaners, handling reschedules, covering callouts

Client communication when things go sideways: a cleaner cancels, a job needs to move, a client is unhappy

Cleaner coordination via Slack and text: confirming schedules, managing no-shows, following up on job quality

Sunday lead data entry and reconciliation



What we're looking for:



It sounds like you might be the kind of person who takes ownership of things. Not because someone told you to, but because that's just how you operate. If something's broken, you fix it. If a lead goes quiet, you follow up again. And again. If a cleaner doesn't show, you don't wait for someone to tell you what to do...you're already calling the client and finding a solution.



Strong spoken and written English. You'll be talking to American homeowners on the phone.

Sales ability. You hear "I need to think about it" and you know what to say next.

Calm under pressure. Three things will go wrong at the same time. That's Tuesday.

Reliability over everything. Show up. Do the work. Log the data. The basics are the job.



Compensation:



$900/month base

$3 bonus per booked client

$100 monthly bonus when your close rate hits 50% or higher

The better you are, the more you earn. A strong performer will clear $1,100/month.

We have 2 pay raises planned in the first 6 months. 



Tools we use: Slack, Seatable, BookingKoala, OpenPhone, Google Workspace



About us: We've been operating since 2023. You'll work directly with the owner and our Cebu-based operations manager. Your results are visible immediately and they matter. If you're looking for a place where what you do actually counts, this is it.



How does this role compare to what you're looking for?

title: Hiring: Experienced Real Estate Assistant
description:
We’re looking for a reliable and experienced Real Estate Cold Caller to join our team!

We require a voice recording for all applicants 

**Real Estate Cold Calling Virtual Assistant (Lead Generation Specialist)**





We are a fast-growing real estate investment company looking for a **highly motivated Virtual Assistant** to help generate **motivated seller leads and connect with serious cash buyers**.



If you are comfortable making **high-volume outbound calls** and enjoy speaking with people, this opportunity is for you.



This role focuses on **lead generation, qualifying motivated sellers, and helping us close more real estate deals.**



**Position Details**



• Pay: **$3.50 per hour**

• Schedule: **6 hours per day (9:30 AM – 3:30 PM PST)**

• Contract: **6 months (with possible extension based on performance)**

• **90-day probationary period**



---



**Daily Responsibilities**



• Make **500–1000 outbound calls per day** to property owners

• Speak with **20–50 homeowners daily**

• Identify and qualify **motivated sellers**

• Ask qualifying questions about the property and situation

• Set appointments with sellers for our acquisitions manager

• Build relationships with **cash buyers and builders**

• Add **serious, verified buyers to spreadsheets**

• Update notes and call outcomes after conversations



---



**Weekly Targets**



• Generate **10–15 qualified seller leads per week**

• Book **5–10 seller appointments per week**

• Help support **10–15 strong offers per week**



---



**Requirements**



• Previous **real estate cold calling experience preferred**

• Strong **English speaking skills**

• Comfortable making **high-volume calls**

• Reliable internet connection and quiet workspace

• Good communication and positive attitude

• Ability to follow scripts and qualify leads properly



---



**We Are Looking For Someone Who**



• Is **consistent and reliable**

• Is comfortable speaking with homeowners

• Is motivated by **performance and results**

• Wants to grow with a real estate investment company



---



**To Apply**



Please include:



1. A **short voice recording introducing yourself**

2. Your **previous cold calling or real estate experience**

3. The dialing system or CRM tools you have used

4. Your **internet speed screenshot**



We are looking to hire **immediately**.

title: : AI-Powered Appointment setting Rockstar / Short term rentals growth manager
description:
Job Title

Salary: $250 USD / Month + Uncapped Bonuses ($5 per "closed" lead)

The Mission: Help me build a multi-million dollar empire across Property Management and Social Lead Generation. I’m from London, and I’m looking for a partner who wants to learn the "London Way"



high-level English, high-level charisma, and high-level results.



The Schedule (London Time):



Mon–Fri: 3 hours/day (Evening shift)



Sunday: 1 hour "Quick-fire" inbox clearing.



Total: 16 hours of high-intensity work per week.



The Role: 50% Appointment setting on Dating apps / 50% Admin Paperwork  Photo Editing AI



The "Dating Assistant" Role (Primary): You will manage Hinge profiles like a pro and dating apps like a pro You’ll use DeepSeek and other AI tools to screenshot and continue the conversation. A/B test which photos and openers get the best response. We are going to build an automated machine where you, optimize the English, and secure dates.



The "Airbnb Specialist" Role: You’ll learn my systems for Airbnb and 
. You’ll be responsible for uploading new listings and ensuring every guest feels like they’re staying in a 5-star hotel. Using a property channel management tools.



The "Daily Strategist": You are my right hand. You plan my day, use AI to automate your own tasks, and keep me "one step ahead" of everything.



Why You?



You are obsessed with AI. You don’t just use it; you want to see how far it can go.



You have a Sales Mindset. You don't just "message"; you "close." you love earning extra!



You are Very Organized. You hate mess and love systems.



You have Ownership. If an Airbnb listing needs a 5-minute fix on a Saturday, you jump on it because you care about the result.



The "Billion Dollar" Perks:



Direct Coaching: I will personally coach you on high-level British English and AI prompting.



Bonuses: I want you to make money when I make money. Every successful appointment you set comes with a cash bonus.



WORKING STYLE:



The 60-Day Sprint: We’ll use Hubstaff for the first 30–60 days as "training wheels." Once you hit your KPIs (e.g., 3 dates/week, 100% admin accuracy), we move to a Results-Based Model. I won't care if you work 1 hour or 3 as long as the results are "billion-dollar level," you have total autonomy and freedom to do your thing.



The Output Agreement: Use AI and automation to your heart's content to speed up your work EXCEPT for sensitive apartment contracts and legal documents. Those tasks must be done manually by you to ensure total privacy and zero errors. So you need to still be able to edit, be good at photoshop / editing manually to an average level.



Mistake Ownership: If a listing error happens, I expect you to jump in for 5–10 mins to fix it, regardless of the hour. Once you master the system, this shouldn't be necessary.







HOW TO APPLY:



Tell me about the hardest project you have done online,



Have you worked in Sales before, or what is the hardest thing you had to sell.

title: Dispatcher
description:
Summary

* Posting Available Equipment - The freight dispatcher will post his truck on the load boards of which he has accounts, he will outline the equipment and time of availability.

* Searching Freight - The freight dispatcher will use the load boards to contact Freight Brokers, Freight Forwarders or Shippers about available loads for your equipment.

* Credit Checks - It is your responsibility to check the credit on the freight broker or shipper prior to accepting the freight. You will do this by using whatever credit service that is available to your carrier such as his factoring company or private credit service.

* Paperwork Transfers - If your carrier has not completed the setup paperwork with the shipper or freight broker already, you will need to fill out the carrier/broker agreement along with any associated payment paperwork and return to the freight broker along with your motor carrier's set up package.

* Insurance Certificates - Contact the carrier’s insurance company and have the freight broker or shipper included as a “Certificate Holder” on the carrier’s insurance policy. All shippers or freight brokers will usually require this to be completed before assigning the load to the carrier.

* Rate Confirmations - Once all the paperwork is transferred to the freight broker or shipper, you will receive a rate confirmation. You will review the details of the rate confirmation for the correct rate and loading orders as agreed, you will then sign and return this to your shipper immediately.

* Driver Dispatch - You will then transfer all information to the carrier for pickups and delivery points and times. I suggest calling the carrier with the load details and also sending a copy of the rate confirmation to his
.

title: Google Organization and Clean Up
description:
Hey there, my name is Tyler and I’m a busy entrepreneur running multiple businesses. I’m looking for a highly organized, proactive virtual assistant who can help me bring structure and clarity to my Google Drive,
 inboxes, and calendar.

Right now, I have multiple Google Drives, multiple inboxes, and a lot of moving pieces. I need someone who can step in, simplify everything, and build clean, easy-to-follow systems that actually work long term. This is not just about organizing once. I’m looking for someone who can help maintain and improve these systems over time.

A big part of this role is understanding how to work with someone who has a fast-moving, idea-heavy mindset. If you’ve ever worked with someone who is highly unorganized or a bit scatter-brained, that’s a big plus. I need someone who can take chaos and turn it into simple, repeatable structure without overcomplicating things.

Here’s what I’m looking for help with:



Organizing multiple Google Drives into clear folder structures

Cleaning up and managing multiple
 inboxes (labels, filters, prioritization)

Helping set up and maintain a simple, effective calendar system

Creating systems that are easy for me to follow and stick to

Ongoing maintenance and improvements as things evolve

Communication is very important to me. I want someone who is not afraid to ask questions, give suggestions, and check in throughout the week to make sure we’re staying aligned and making progress.

If this sounds like something that would interest you, please send me a short introduction video explaining why you think you’d be a great fit for this role.

Bonus points if you’ve worked with highly unorganized or scatter-brained individuals before. If so, I’d love for you to briefly explain what challenges you saw and what systems or solutions you put in place to help them get organized and stay consistent.

If it seems like a good fit, I’d love to schedule a call and talk through the next steps.

Looking forward to hearing from you.

title: Customer Support Associate (Off-Hours Coverage)
description:
APPLICATION LINK: 

NOTE: applicants who do not complete the application form above will not be considered.



Role Overview

The Customer Support Associate (Off-Hours Coverage) role exists to make sure Coast customers are never left without support — including evenings and weekends. You'll be the primary point of contact during off-hours, resolving customer inquiries, troubleshooting product issues, and delivering a high-quality experience across
, chat, and phone.



Because ticket volume varies throughout the shift, this role also takes on meaningful project and operational work during quieter windows — from improving our help center content to supporting billing and internal operations. You'll develop a broad understanding of how Coast runs as a business, which makes this role an excellent foundation for advancement into Implementation, Customer Success, or other roles over time.



Working hours: This is an off-hours coverage role supporting U.S.-based customers. Schedules vary by position, but both openings cover weekends and weekday evenings EST. See shift details below.



Rep 1 - 40 hours

Saturday: 8:00 AM - 6:00 PM (9:00 PM - 7:00 AM Sunday)

Sunday - Tuesday, 6:00 PM - 4:00 AM EST (7:00 AM – 5:00 PM PHT)



Rep 2 - 40 hours

Wednesday – Saturday, 5:00 PM - 3:00 AM EST (6:00 AM – 4:00 PM PHT)



Rep 3 - 30 hours

Sunday, 8:00 AM - 6:00 PM EST (9:00 PM - 7:00 AM Monday PHT)

Monday - Wednesday, 4:00 AM - 9:00 AM EST ( 5:00 PM - 10:00 PM) 



Please apply only if you are available and comfortable working these hours. Night shift differential is included in compensation.



Key Responsibilities

Responsibilities are prioritized in order of importance. Customer support is always the first priority; project work fills quieter windows.



1) Customer support 

- Respond to customer inquiries via
, chat, and phone in a timely, accurate, and empathetic manner during your shift.

- Troubleshoot product and account issues using knowledge base articles, SOPs, and good judgment; escalate complex cases when needed.

- Monitor and manage your ticket queue to meet SLAs for first response and resolution.

- Document all customer interactions, resolutions, and follow-ups in Salesforce and Intercom/Zendesk to maintain a clear history.

- Collaborate with the US-based Implementation and Customer Success teams on customer handoffs, flagging urgent issues for next-day follow-up when appropriate.



2) Systems & data support 

- Own customer data intake, cleanup, validation, and ongoing maintenance to ensure accuracy and readiness for implementation and reporting.

- Execute customer data imports and migrations (primarily via spreadsheets and templates), troubleshoot issues, and ensure clean handoffs to Implementation and Customer Success.

- Document work, decisions, and customer configurations in internal systems (Salesforce, Intercom/Zendesk, and project tools) to maintain clear visibility and continuity.

- Identify patterns and opportunities to improve data templates, processes, and internal documentation to increase speed and consistency across implementations.



3) General operational and administrative support 

- During quieter support windows, contribute to a rotating set of operational projects assigned by the team. Examples include:

- Help center and content: Review and revise help desk articles for accuracy and clarity; create short help videos and walkthroughs to support customer self-service; suggest FAQ updates based on ticket patterns.

- Billing support: Monitor accounts receivable aging and send structured payment reminder
s; track upcoming subscription renewals and send timely notices; triage the billing inbox during off-hours and respond to routine billing questions; support basic data entry in Stripe and Salesforce.

- General admin and research: Assist with internal operational tasks across teams — including HR admin support, basic research, and other cross-functional work as assigned.



What This Role Can Lead To

This position is intentionally designed as a foundation for growth at Coast. As you develop deep product knowledge and cross-functional exposure, you'll be well-positioned to move into roles such as:

- Implementation Specialist — configuring Coast for new customers and supporting go-lives

- Customer Success Manager — owning customer relationships and driving retention

- Systems & Data Associate — managing customer data, imports, and workflow configuration



We promote from within and will actively support your development toward whichever path fits your strengths.



Requirements

- At least 1 year of customer support experience, preferably in a SaaS or tech environment.

- Excellent written and verbal English communication skills — clear, friendly, and professional.

- Comfortable working independently during off-hours with minimal real-time oversight.

- Available and willing to work the shift hours listed above, including weekends and Philippine overnight hours.

- Comfortable working daily in our tech stack, including Coast, Google Workspace, Zoo
et, Salesforce, and support/project tools such as Zendesk, Intercom, and Avoma (or similar equivalents). Previous experience with these tools is a plus.

- Strong attention to detail and follow-through. 

- Organized, self-directed, and able to switch between support and project work within the same shift.

- Reliable high?speed internet connection and a professional, quiet workspace suitable for phone calls, video calls, and screen?sharing.

- Basic familiarity with billing concepts (invoices, subscriptions, payment reminders) is a plus, not required.



We are NOT able to support work visas at this time.



Training, Onboarding, and Probation

We invest heavily in setting you up to succeed. Here's what your first 90 days look like:



Weeks 1–4: Core Training - You'll go through a structured four-week training program combining self-paced learning with live sessions and regular group check-ins with your cohort. By the end, you'll have a strong foundation in Coast's platform, our customer base, and how we work.



Weeks 5–12: Job Training - Training wheels come off gradually. You'll move into hands-on job training where you begin managing your own implementations under guidance — learning by doing with real customers and real stakes.



90-Day Probation Period - Your first 90 days serve as a mutual evaluation period. We want to make sure this is the right fit for you as much as for us, and we'll give you clear expectations, regular feedback, and the support you need to hit the ground running. Successful completion leads to a longer-term engagement with Coast.



What We Offer You 

- The Startup Experience: Shaping Our Growth: Joining Coast at this pivotal stage puts you at the heart of our company’s and product’s evolution. The workplace is energetic, creative, and fast-moving, giving you the chance to help define processes and standards while collaborating directly with founders and teammates who value bold thinking and active input. Here, tea
mbers aren’t just part of the journey—they shape it, building and innovating with visible impact every single day.



- Team: We are a tight knit and very strong core team of ~40 people. We are a highly customer focused, honest, skilled, direct, solution oriented, creative, ambitious, and fun crew. Previously we’ve worked at big companies like Adobe, Amazon, Meta, and Microsoft and at both early and late stage for startups like 
, HotelTonight, UpKeep, Latch, and Fitbit. We solve tough customer problems together and continually help one another level up.



- Product: The market we are going after is massive and way underserved. Our customers love the product and use it for hours every day. What you will be building will directly impact their everyday life. We use Coast as our central nervous system just like our customers!



Contract Details

- Engagement type: Independent contractor based in the Philippines; taxes and statutory contributions are the responsibility of the contractor. No health benefits included.

- Payment currency and method: USD via bank transfer, paid monthly.

- Working hours: As listed above. Night shift differential included in compensation.



Compensation

Base pay of $15,000 - $20,000 USD annually for full time consulting. 



----

About Coast

Coast is a no-code maintenance and work order platform built specifically for the deskless workforce. Available on Android, iOS, and web, our software is designed for teams on the move—making it easy to stay connected and on top of daily operations, no matter where work happens.



By combining real-time chat with customizable maintenance workflows, Coast helps teams streamline communication, coordinate tasks, and get more done with less friction.



While Coast is flexible enough to support a wide variety of operational needs, our current focus is on solving critical maintenance challenges for organizations that rely on speed, clarity, and accountability.

title: AI-Powered E-Commerce Specialist — Shopify + WordPress + AI Workflows
description:
AI-POWERED E-COMMERCE SPECIALIST

Shopify + WordPress + AI Workflows — Build Pages & Systems That Scale

 

Remote | Full-time | Philippines

$1,400–$1,800/month starting + raise after 3 months based on performance

 

 

WHO WE ARE

 

We're a European e-commerce incubator. We test, validate, and scale consumer brands across 12+ countries. Small team (under 10 people), fully remote, moving fast. Two established brands in pet products and health supplements, growing 2–3x every year — with plans to launch 20+ new brands in 2026.

 

We're an AI-first company. Claude, ChatGPT, Midjourney — we use them every day to move faster than teams 5x our size. We don't just "allow" AI. We expect everyone on the team to actively use it. If you're someone who's already building with AI, you'll fit right in.

 

 

THE ROLE

 

This role has two sides. First: you build our front-end sales infrastructure — Shopify stores, WordPress landing pages, product graphics. Second: you build AI-powered processes and systems that let us do this at scale across 12+ markets.

 

You work directly with our CMO (your day-to-day manager) and CEO. No layers. No bureaucracy.

 

This is NOT a "general VA" role where you answer
s and do data entry. And it's not a pure "page builder" role either. You're someone who builds AND thinks about how to build faster, cheaper, and at higher volume using AI.

 

The role starts execution-heavy. As you prove yourself, it expands into building automated workflows, running AI-driven competitor analysis, and coordinating our brand incubation pipeline.

 

 

WHAT YOU'LL DO

 

Building pages & funnels (~40–50% of your time)

 

• Build Shopify product pages from briefs — Create full Shopify stores from A to Z: images, descriptions, variants, pricing, CTAs. Use AI to generate product descriptions, optimize copy, and create variations faster.

 

• Build WordPress/Elementor landing pages — Format articles, add product images, CTA buttons. Use AI for content formatting, image generation, and responsive adjustments.

 

• Duplicate proven funnels to new markets — Clone working funnels, swap translated content. Use AI to check translations and adapt copy for local markets. Repeat across 12+ countries.

 

• Maintain and update existing pages — price changes, image swaps, link fixes, seasonal updates. You're the single owner of our front-end.

 

AI-powered graphics & creative production (~20–30% of your time)

 

• Create product mockups and visuals using AI — Midjourney, DALL-E, or similar for product shots, lifestyle images, packaging visuals. Canva and Photoshop for finishing and formatting.

 

• Build a system for high-volume ad creative production — Set up AI workflows to rapidly produce ad variations: different headlines, images, layouts. The goal is 50+ creative variants per campaign, not 5.

 

• Competitor creative analysis — Use Meta Ads Library + AI to systematically copy and analyze what's working for competitors. Build a swipe file and create our own versions faster.

 

• Design banners, comparison graphics, and page assets — use AI to generate base images, then refine for brand consistency.

 

AI processes, tracking & automation (~20–30% of your time)

 

• Build AI-powered workflows that save hours — Set up repeatable processes: AI-assisted product description generation, automated image creation pipelines, translation checking systems. Document everything so it scales.

 

• Set up ad creative production pipelines — Create systems where we can input a brief and output 20+ ad variations using AI. Think templates + AI generation + human polish.

 

• Implement tracking on every new page —
 Pixel, Google Tag Manager, conversion tracking. No page goes live without proper tracking.

 

• AI-assisted competitor research — Use AI + our playbook to scan Meta Ads Library, Amazon, Google Trends. Deliver structured briefs for CEO decision-making.

 

• Keep our pipeline moving in Notion — update task statuses, track deadlines, flag blockers.

 

 

WHO WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 

You must have:

 

• You actively use AI tools in your work — ChatGPT, Claude, Midjourney, or similar. Not "I've tried it once" — you use it regularly to get work done faster. In your application, give us one concrete example of how AI helped you complete a task.

 

• Shopify experience — building product pages, not just managing orders. Show us 2–3 pages you've built.

 

• WordPress + Elementor proficiency — you can build a full landing page from a brief. Responsive, clean, fast-loading.

 

• Graphic design skills — Canva (required), Photoshop or AI design tools (preferred). You create product mockups and page visuals.

 

• You understand how e-commerce funnels work — why comparison pages convert, why CTAs matter, and how product pages drive purchases. You don't need to be a copywriter, but you get the logic behind selling online.

 

• Process thinking — you don't just do the task, you think about how to do it 10x faster next time. You build templates, checklists, and workflows.

 

• Basic HTML/CSS — enough to fix spacing, fonts, colors without waiting for a developer.

 

• Google Tag Manager basics — install tags, set up triggers, verify tracking.

 

• Strong English (B2+) — you'll read detailed briefs and communicate async via Slack. Comprehension must be flawless.

 

• Self-directed work style — you read the brief, execute, deliver. You ask clarifying questions upfront, not halfway through.

 

• We work hard and pay well. This is a 44h/week role, including ~4 hours on Saturdays. The rest of your schedule is flexible around core hours.

 

Nice to have:

 

• Experience with multilingual/multi-market e-commerce (EU markets preferred)

 

• You've built AI-assisted workflows or automations (Zapier, Make, custom GPTs, n8n)

 

• Prompt engineering skills — you know how to get consistent, high-quality results from AI tools

 

• Experience creating ad creatives at scale 
/Instagram/Google Ads)

 

• Notion experience (databases, kanban boards, task management)

 

• Video editing or AI-generated content experience

 

 

WHAT WE OFFER

 

• $1,400–$1,800/month starting salary (based on experience)

 

• 13th month bonus based on performance

 

• Performance bonuses with a clear path to $1,900+ if you deliver

 

• Monthly health allowance included

 

• 10 days paid leave per year

 

• Paid 2-week trial — we both evaluate fit before committing long-term

 

• 100% remote work

 

• Flexible schedule — core overlap 2:00–6:00 PM Manila (8:00 AM–12:00 PM CET), rest is flexible

 

• Direct work with CMO and CEO

 

• AI tools and subscriptions provided — we invest in the tools you need to work fast

 

• Growth trajectory — start with execution, grow into AI systems building and strategy support

 

• Stable, long-term position — we're profitable and growing. This isn't a 3-month gig.

 

• Monthly payment via Wise

 

 

HIRING PROCESS

 

1. Online Test — You will receive a link to a 10 min online test

 

2. Paid test task — build a real product page or landing page from our brief. 48h deadline. We always pay for test work.

 

3. Video call with CMO or CEO (30–60 min) — your future manager. We talk about your experience, how you use AI, and how you work.

 

4. 2-week paid trial — full onboarding, real tasks, mutual evaluation.

 

5. Full-time offer — if we're both happy, you're in.

 

 

WHAT THIS ROLE IS NOT

 

• "General VA" who does
s, data entry, and social media — this is specialized e-commerce + AI work

 

• "Designer" who never sees results — you see exactly how your pages perform

 

• "Project manager" who coordinates but doesn't build — you build everything yourself

 

• Someone who "knows about AI" but doesn't actually use it — we need hands-on, daily AI usage

 

• A "prompt engineer" who only talks to ChatGPT — you build real pages and graphics, AI just makes you faster

 

• A short-term freelance gig — we want someone long-term who grows with us

 

 

We're looking for someone who builds fast, thinks in systems, and uses AI as a daily superpower.

If this sounds like you — apply now.
